data.title: Aaron
data.definition: Aaron is a Hebrew male name that means 'exalted'. There are interpretations of the name that suggest the meaning is 'teacher' or 'mountain of strength'. This is a great name for a pet that is strong in mind and in build.

data.title: Abby
data.definition: Originally a Hebrew name with biblical origins, Abby means 'father's joy'. Perfect for any daddy's girls!

data.title: Abu
data.definition:
The name Abu is a male name of African origin that means 'Nobility'. It is also often used as a nickname as it can also mean 'father of' in Indian culture.

The name Abu is most famous as the mischevious pet monkey of Aladdin!

data.title: Ace
data.definition: As confident a name as a person might be with one of these cards up their sleeve, Ace is actually of Latin origin and means 'One' or 'Expert'. It's a great short, sharp name for the special 'one' in your life.

data.title: Achilles
data.definition: The hero of Homer’s Iliad, Achilles was a warrior known for having a weakness in his heel which eventually caused his demise. Although the name itself comes from the Greek word meaning 'pain', we'd rather focus on the namesake's legendary strength and bravery!

data.title: Acorn
data.definition: The name is derived from the German word 'eichorn', which is a place where squirrels are in abundance. Acorn might make a lovely name for dogs with a brown coat, or little dogs!

data.title: Ada
data.definition: Ada has its origin in German culture and means ‘happiness’ and ‘noble’. The name originally was a shortened version of the name Adelaide.

data.title: Addie
data.definition: Meaning 'noble' in both its original Latin and German forms, Addie can also mean 'sweet'.

data.title: Aero
data.definition: Whilst we might immediately think of the airy and bubbly chocolate bar, Aero is actually a name of Greek origin and means 'of the sky'.

data.title: Aether
data.definition: Aether means brightness and light in Ancient Greek, and according to medieval science is also considered to be the 5th element of alchemical chemistry - the stuff of stars!

data.title: Ai
data.definition: Originating in Japan, the name Ai, is a lovely female name. There are two meanings associated with this name, those being ‘love’ and ‘indigo blue’. If you have a pup who is super loving towards others, then this is a great name to choose. It might also be an unusual choice for pups with blue eyes, like the Siberian Husky, Border Collie or even the odd Dachshund! You might even have one of the rarer dog breeds with a blue coat - find out more about them here.

data.title: Aidan
data.definition: 'Meaning 'little fire', Aidan is also taken from Aodh, the Celtic sun god . No surprise then that this is a great match for little pets with big personalities; the perfect match for a Dachshund, Japanese Spitz or Pomeranian.

data.title: Aiden
data.definition: 'Meaning 'little fire', Aidan is also taken from Aodh, the Celtic sun god . No surprise then that this is a great match for little pets with big personalities; the perfect match for a Dachshund, Japanese Spitz or Pomeranian.

data.title: Aladdin
data.definition: Famous for being a daring street urchin and love interest of Princess Jasmine in the Disney film Aladdin, this male name means 'excellence' and 'nobility'.

data.title: Alba
data.definition: The name Alba is Gaelic for 'Scotland', and was originally of Latin origin meaning 'white', 'clear' or 'bright'. A pretty name for the West Highland White Terrier, or Westie, a classic Scottish breed.

data.title: Albert
data.definition: Famous for being the name of Einstein and Queen Victoria's husband, Albert is a name of German origin that means 'bright'.

data.title: Albus
data.definition: You don’t need to be a Harry Potter fanatic to know who this is, but did you know Albus actually means 'white' in Latin too? For the pure-hearted pets which of course, is all of them.

data.title: Alden
data.definition: Alden is a male name of British origin, and means 'old friend'.

data.title: Alex
data.definition: There's a lot riding on the name Alex as it means 'defender of mankind' from its Greek origin! The name is derived from the Greek name Alexandros, which comes from the Greek word alexein which means 'to defend' or 'to protect' - definitely a good choice if your dog is the guardian of the household!

data.title: Alexis
data.definition: Apart from the popular Schitt's Creek character, Alexis comes from the Greek word meaning 'helper' or 'defender'. A great name for your own sidekick!

data.title: Alf
data.definition: Alfie is a male name of Old English origin, that means 'elf'. The name is derived from the name Aelfraed, and might be suited to any mischief-makers coming into your household!

data.title: Alfie
data.definition: Alfie is a male name of Old English origin, that means 'elf'. The name is derived from the name Aelfraed, and might be suited to any mischief-makers coming into your household!

data.title: Alice
data.definition: The most famous Alice might be Lewis Carroll's Wonderland heroine; though originally the name came from the German meaning 'noble'. Classical, with a touch of mischief!

data.title: Allegra
data.definition: A name of Itialian origin, Allegra is a female name meaning 'joyful' and 'lively'. If you have a pup that brings huge happiness to your life, and whose tail never stops wagging, then this is the perfect name for them.

data.title: Allie
data.definition: First derived from 'Allison', this shorter form is perfect for pups and means 'nobility' or 'harmony' and has been around since medieval times.

data.title: Alma
data.definition: Alma has its roots in lots of languages, but its meaning can be linked to the word 'soul' which is a lovely name for gentle-spirited pups.

data.title: Almond
data.definition: An unusual choice of name for smaller pooches, Almond might be a good match for nutty little personalities!

data.title: Alta
data.definition: Alta comes from the old Latin meaning 'high', or 'elevated', so we think it would make a cool name for tall dogs like Greyhounds, Wolfhounds or Great Danes! Find out more about giant dog breeds here.

data.title: Alyssa
data.definition: As pretty as the flower it's derived from, the 'alyssum', the name also has a serious side and comes from the Greek meaning 'rational'.

data.title: Amber
data.definition: A great name for dogs with this distinctive, jewel eye-colour like the Weimeraner, Dachshund or Husky, Amber also means 'fierce' in its Arabic roots and 'great joy' from its English origin.

data.title: Andromeda
data.definition: This female name is of Greek origin, and means 'leader of humankind' - so not a name for the faint-hearted! For Greek mythology lovers, you will know that Andromeda was the daughter of Cassiopeia, who much like her mother, became a constellation. Andromeda is also the name of the nearest major galaxy to the Milky Way; an unusual name for the star in your life!

data.title: Angel
data.definition: Of course we all think our dogs are angels; as perfect as the heavenly beings this name comes from. The Greek 'Angelos' means 'heavenly messenger'.

data.title: Anna
data.definition: Meaning 'favour' or 'grace' from the original Hebrew, Anna is a classical name for tradition-lovers - or lovers of the Disney film Frozen!

data.title: Annie
data.definition: A variation of the name 'Anna', Annie means 'grace' but might also remind you of the flame-haired orphan from the musical. Perhaps the perfect name for an adoptee pet?

data.title: Aphrodite
data.definition: The Greek meaning of the name Aphrodite is 'risen from the sea', and is given to the Greek goddess of love and beauty making this a very regal name for the loves of your pawrenting life.

data.title: Apollo
data.definition: Apollo is one of the most noted names of Greek mythology and although it means 'destroyer' (and lets face it, what puppy hasn't taken apart a sofa leg or two?), he was also known as the God of the sun, music, poetry and medicine.

data.title: Apple
data.definition: This is a lovely gender neutral name of British origin, simply meaning 'The apple fruit'. The most famous use of this name was for Gwyneth Paltrow and Chris Martin's daughter, Apple Martin.

data.title: Archer
data.definition: Get your bow and arrow ready! Archer is an English boys name, dervied from the Latin surname of Middle Ages archers. The name means 'bowman'. In the 19th century the name became a more popular choice for a first name.

data.title: Archie
data.definition: Archie is a name of Scottish origin, and is the shortened name of Archibald. The name Archie means 'archer' or 'bold'. The name has come into the spotlight after Prince Harry and Meghan Markle named their first child this!

data.title: Argon
data.definition: Argon may be best known as the noble gas from your periodic table lessons, but did you know it comes from the Greek meaning 'lazy'? It's no wonder chemists haven't been able to combine it with any other elements! A fun and unusual name for sofa-surfers who prefer some shut-eye over a walk.

data.title: Argus
data.definition: Argus comes from the Greek and refers to a 'watchful guardian'. In mythology it refers to a creature with hundreds of eyes, which was later changed into a beautiful peacock with its all-seeing tail feathers! An interesting choice for sighthounds like Whippets, Greyhounds and Salukis.

data.title: Ariel
data.definition: Of course you'll know Ariel as Disney's beautiful mermaid princess; but did you know the original name means 'lion of God' from the Hebrew origin?

data.title: Aries
data.definition: Aries is a boy's name of Latin origin meaning "a ram" and of course, reflects the zodiac sign of the same name. The name is derived of the Roman god of war, representing strength and bravery.

data.title: Artemis
data.definition: Artemis is the Greek goddess of hunting, known for her wild spirit, protection of girls and association to the moon. An impressive name for traditional hunting breeds like Pointers, Setters and Retrievers.

data.title: Artie
data.definition: Artie is a boys name of Celtic origin that means 'bear'. It is usually used as nickname or short form of the name Arthur.

data.title: Arya
data.definition: Arya was originally a Sanskrit name meaning 'noble' or 'air'. It's become best known as the name of the youngest sister of the Stark family in Game of Thrones, known for her fierce independence and stubborn nature! She's tough, cunning and determined; a strong name for a loyal companion!

data.title: Ash
data.definition: Ash is derived from the name Asher, meaning 'happy'. Of course it's also the name of a common tree; Vikings believed the Ash was a symbol of protection, so this might make a good name for guardians of the home!

data.title: Ashley
data.definition: A lovely name for nature lovers, as Ashley originally came from the Old English words for 'ash' and 'forest glade'.

data.title: Aska
data.definition: Aska comes from the Old Norse for "ashes", and would make an unusual choice for dog breeds with a grey coat.

data.title: Aslan
data.definition: Aslan is probably best known as the lion from '[The Lion, The Witch, and The Wardrobe', but did you know that the name Aslan, comes from the Turkish for ‘lion’? A great name for dogs with a big voice or, a tongue-in-cheek choice for little lions with big personalities.

data.title: Aspen
data.definition: The name of a famous ski resort in Colorado, this might be a great choice for cold-weather dogs like Huskys, Malamutes or Newfoundlands. It's also the name of a type of tree with heart-shaped leaves so delicate they're also known as 'quaking trees'.

data.title: Asta
data.definition: We love this Norse name meaning 'divine strength', 'love' and even 'bright as a star'. If your pup is the centre of your celestial world, this might be the perfect name for you.

data.title: Aster
data.definition: Aster is the Greek word for 'star', making it a celestial choice for the main character in your life! It's also the name of a daisy-like purple flower symbolising wisdom and faith.

data.title: Astrid
data.definition: Astrid, an old Scandinavian name that means ‘divinely beautiful’. This is a traditional Norse name that comes from the old Norse word ‘Ástriõr’, which is made up of two words, ‘áss’ meaning god, and ‘friõr’ meaning beautiful.

data.title: Astro
data.definition: Astro is the name of the futuristic Jetson's dog, known for his attention-seeking antics and undying love for his master George. Short for 'astrology' or 'astronomy', it also makes a cool name for fans of space exploration!

data.title: Athena
data.definition: Athena is the Greek Goddess of Wisdom, born fully grown and armoured; a great name for the cleverest breeds like Border Collies, Poodles and Labradors.

data.title: Atlas
data.definition: Atlas is the name of the Titan who bore the weight of the world on his shoulders. The name translates to “bearer of the heavens”. This name would be a great option if you have a pup that takes the world in their stride, or if they are your adventure buddy that you take everywhere with you!

data.title: Aura
data.definition: This ethereal name has its Latin meaning in 'glowing light', and was also the Greek Titan goddess of breezes who was transformed into a fountain by Zeus. A fresh, unusual name.

data.title: Aurora
data.definition:
Aurora is a female name that refers to the natural light that is displayed in the Earth's sky known as the aurora polaris, or polar lights, which are only visible in high-altitude regions such as the North and South Pole regions. The name means 'dawn' in Latin.

Namesake for one of the most famous Disney princesses, Princess Aurora, also known as sleeping beauty. Perfect for the pup that brings light into your life - or loves to snooze!

data.title: Autumn
data.definition: A seasonal name from the Latin meaning 'of woods', this might be a pretty match for auburn-coloured pets like the Irish Setter, Vizsla or darker Golden Retrievers.

data.title: Ava
data.definition: This little name has lots of meanings; including the Latin 'bird', Persian 'voice', Germanic 'island' or Old English 'life'.

data.title: Avery
data.definition: Fans of Lord of the Rings might like this name, meaning 'ruler of the elves'. Originally a male name, today it has become more common for girls.

data.title: Baby
data.definition: This one is self-explanatory; if your pet is your baby, why not say so! Fans of Dirty Dancing might also like the reference to this classic 80's dance drama's leading lady.

data.title: Bagheera
data.definition: Bagheera, famously known as the tritagonist Panther from The Jungle Book, is primarily a male name of Indian origin, meaning 'tiger'.

data.title: Bai
data.definition: If you have a white dog and are looking for something a little unusual, we have you covered. There are some lovely names out there, but if you want a name that is a bit off-grid for your pup, then this is the name for you. Bai, originating from Taoist culture, means both ‘pure’ and ‘outgoing’. A great choice for gregarious pups with snowy coats!

data.title: Bailey
data.definition: From the Old English words for 'bailiff' or 'steward', Bailey might be a good match for a peace-loving but protective pooch like Akitas, German Shepherds or Doberman Pinschers.

data.title: Baloo
data.definition: Baloo, famously known as the kind bear from The Jungle Book, is a name of Norman origin. The name simply translates to bear. If you have a friendly giant in your house, then this is the perfect name for them.

data.title: Balto
data.definition: This is a name with a great story being it. Balto was a real-life sled dog known for leading his sled-pulling team through the toughest of conditions to deliver medicine to a village that was in desperate need of saving in 1925. A name that represents courage, bravery and strength and a meaningful choice for Huskys or Malamutes.

data.title: Bandit
data.definition: If you've got a naughty pup with a penchant for thieving socks, toys or even snacks, this might be a good fit for their mischievious character.

data.title: Bantam
data.definition: The name Bantam is of English origin, and is said to have been founded in Northumberland. The place name literally means 'estate associated with a man called Bada'. It's also known as a breed of chicken!

data.title: Bao
data.definition: Do you have a pup that you treasure with your whole heart? We have just the name for you! Bao is a unisex name that means ‘treasure’. Of course it's also the name of a tasty steamed bun, so perfect for any little dumplings in your life too!

data.title: Barkley
data.definition: Barkley is a male name of English origin. No, it doesn’t have any meaning of barking too much, although it may be a great name choice if your pup does bark a lot! The name means 'birch tree meadow'.

data.title: Barley
data.definition: Barley is a lovely English name, unsurprisingly connected to those who grew or sold barley, and actually comes from the Old English for 'wild boar meadow'.

data.title: Barney
data.definition: Barney is a Hebrew name that means 'son of comfort', though its Old English and German roots mean 'strong as a bear' and 'defender'. That being said, Barney has been famously used for the purple Dinosaur and for Barney Gumble in The Simpsons, so it's a fun, flexible option for your pup!

data.title: Baron
data.definition: Baron is a boy's name of Hebrew origin, and it means 'young warrior'. This is a great name choice for those pups that are little warriors and love a good adventure.

data.title: Basil
data.definition: A clown-like personality might suit Basil for its reference to the owner of Fawlty Towers, even though the name originally comes from the Greek meaning 'royal' and 'brave', and the Arabic for 'fearless'! Of course there's also the Italian herb perfect for pizza-lovers!

data.title: Bea
data.definition: This female name meaning 'bringer of happiness', is a timeless name of American origin. Primarily used as a nickname of Beatrice, Bea is becoming a beautiful given name on its own.

data.title: Bean
data.definition: A cute one for little pups, Bean also comes from the Middle English meaning 'friendly'! If you're a fan of coffee beans, baked beans or Mexican black beans, this could be the name for you.

data.title: Beast
data.definition: Beast is usually thought of as a rather grizzly male name, and became famous for the Disney film 'Beauty and the Beast'. Although we are not suggesting your pet is a Beast, the name might simply imply that they are a strong and brave pup!

data.title: Beau
data.definition: Beau is a male name of French origin. It was traditionally used as the nickname for the word beautiful, before becoming a name in its own right.

data.title: Beethoven
data.definition: Star of the 90s classic film Beethoven, this larger-than-life St Bernard was a slobbery, energetic addition to his adopted family, and the name is well-suited to big breeds with even bigger hearts. Of course Beethoven was also the name of one of Germany's most revered composers.

data.title: Bella
data.definition: A gorgeous Italian name meaning 'beautiful', Bella is also known as the heroine of Stephenie Meyer's Twilight novels - the perfect name if you love reading or all things beautiful!

data.title: Belle
data.definition: Straight from the language of love, Belle is French for 'beautiful', and of course the namesake of Disney's princess in Beauty and the Beast!

data.title: Benji
data.definition: Benji was the name of a TV terrier made famous for stealing hearts in the 70s; it's got a little nostalgia and a little naughtiness about it!

data.title: Bentley
data.definition: Most famous for the luxurious brand of cars, the name Bentley actually means, 'meadow with coarse grass'. Bentley is a great name choice for pups that loving the rolling hills of the countryside, or rolling around passenger-side taking in the sights!

data.title: Berlioz
data.definition: Berlioz is a male name of French origin, it is not commonly used as a given name, but as a surname. The name means 'just'. It is famous for being the name of the French composer, who was regarded as a pioneer of modern orchestration, as well as the gray-furred kitten in Disney's The Aristocats.

data.title: Bert
data.definition: This is a great name for those diamond white pups. Bert is primarily a male name of German origin that means Bright. Made famous by Disney's Mary Poppins and the chimney-sweeping Bert.

data.title: Bertha
data.definition: The name Bertha is girl's name of German origin. Bertha is one of the most popular girls names, and it means 'bright' and 'glorious'.

data.title: Betsy
data.definition: The 'pet name' of 'Elizabeth', Betsy comes from the Hebrew meaning 'God is perfection'. And God backwards is after all, dog!

data.title: Betty
data.definition: The 'pet name' of 'Elizabeth', Betsy comes from the Hebrew meaning 'God is my oath'. And God backwards is after all, dog!

data.title: Bianca
data.definition: From the Italian meaning 'pure' or 'white', Bianca might be perfect for snowy-hued pups like Samoyeds, Pomeranians or Maltese. A favourite of Shakespeare, Bianca features in both Othello and The Taming of the Shrew.

data.title: Bingo
data.definition: "Bingo was his NAME-OH" is a famous nursery rhyme, and the name of course also comes from the fun numbers game, making it an all-round cheerful choice for any pet! Get your Bingo-calling practice in during recall training with this name.

data.title: Birdie
data.definition: A shorter version of 'Bernice' or 'Bertha', Birdie has a vintage vibe meaning 'little bird', 'birdlike' and 'bright'.

data.title: Biscuit
data.definition: This fun, unusual name for lovers of the British treat is sure to turn heads and raise a smile when called across the park!

data.title: Blanco
data.definition: A cute Spanish name for white dogs like Samoyeds, Maltese or West Highland White Terriers with a little pep in their step!

data.title: Blaze
data.definition: Blaze unsurprisingly means 'to shine brightly' and can refer to fire and flame, making it a sparky name for golden or red-furred pups, or just those with a firey personality!

data.title: Blink
data.definition: This super easy and snappy one syllable name for your dog is great to make your dog stand out! The name "Blink" is a unique and playful name and whilst it doesn't have a specific meaning in the traditional sense, it evokes a sense of quickness, agility, and liveliness.

data.title: Blondie
data.definition: Music-lovers will know Blondie from hits like Heart of Glass and Call Me. If your pup's a punk rocker in spirit, or just a pale-furred beauty, this might be the name for you.

data.title: Blossom
data.definition: A sweet spring name meaning 'to bloom' or 'flower-like', Blossom might fit a delicate breed like the Chihuahua, Yorkshire Terrier or Maltese.

data.title: Blue
data.definition: Of course Blue is a well-loved colour, bringing to mind everything from the sky to the ocean. So, whether you have a grey-blue dog breed or one with blue eyes, this is a great name for them!

data.title: Bolt
data.definition: Ready to hear that sonic bark? Bolt is a famous dog name from the Disney film Bolt. The name Bolt means 'lightning' and 'flash'. So, if you have a pup that runs at the speed of light, this is the name for you!

data.title: Bonnie
data.definition: The Scots often refer to their beautiful home as 'Bonnie Scotland' because the word means 'pretty' and 'cheerful'. For a more rebellious pet you could also take inspiration from outlaw Bonnie in Bonnie and Clyde.

data.title: Brandy
data.definition: The Dutch origin of the name comes from the alcoholic drink, meaning 'burnt wine, whilst the Italian meaning is 'sword'. The drink's rich caramel hues means it might be a lovely match for Great Danes, Chow Chows or German Shepherds.

data.title: Breeze
data.definition: Breeze is of English origin and means 'a light' and 'gentle wind', and might be suited to a calm-natured dog.

data.title: Brody
data.definition: Brody is an Irish name that means 'ditch' or 'muddy place'. This would be a great name for a brown pup, or one that like to hurl themselves into water at any opportunity!

data.title: Bronte
data.definition: A classical-sounding name which could either refer to the English writer Emily Bronte, or to the more dramatic Greek origin meaning 'thunder'!

data.title: Brooklyn
data.definition: Perhaps best known as one of New York's boroughs, the original English meaning of the name is 'beautiful brook' and of course, it's one of the famous Beckham's sons names.

data.title: Brownie
data.definition: This popular chocolatey treat might be a great fit for sweet-natured brown-coloured companions including the less known brown Labradors and liver Flatcoats.

data.title: Buddy
data.definition: Meaning 'friend', this is a great name to give your pup to signify the best of bonds between you.

data.title: Buffy
data.definition: Buffy is actually another pet name for 'Elizabeth', but is probably better known as the fierce female vampire slayer from the TV series and movie.

data.title: Buster
data.definition: Buster is a male name of American origin, with 'tough guy' connotations. Perfect for rough and tumble pups with hearts of gold.

data.title: Busy
data.definition: The name Busy is actually a rare nickname form of Elizabeth, and we love it as a choice for an active pup full of energy!

data.title: Buttercup
data.definition: This is a floral girls name of English origin meaning, 'yellow wildflower'. However, this name not only represents a beautiful yellow flower, but also that of royalty, as the name Buttercup was given to the princess in the 1987 film The Princess Bride. A lovely, cheerful name that feels full of sunshine!

data.title: Caesar
data.definition: Of course Caesar is best known as arguably the most famous Roman Emperor, but did you know the name actually means 'thick head of hair'? Making it a great name for both pups who are natural-born leaders, or those who are just really, really fluffy!

data.title: Calinda
data.definition: A name with steeped in Hindu mythology, Kalinda. The name Kalinda, means ‘sun’. The name also shares a mythological meaning to the mountains of Kalinda or the sacred Kalindi river. Pets are the sunshine of our lives, and this name is a great option to show just that.

data.title: Calla
data.definition: Calla means 'beautiful' in its Greek form, which makes sense as it also refers to the pretty Calla Lilly. A sweet, delicate option which is a little more distinctive than Lily.

data.title: Callie
data.definition: From the Greek name Callista, Callie means 'most beautiful' and can also be a shortened form of Caroline.

data.title: Calliope
data.definition: Calliope was the first of the nine Greek muses and in charge of epic poetry, so it's no surprise the name means 'beautiful voice'. One for the barkers, howlers and yappers out there!

data.title: Camilla
data.definition: In Italian Camilla means 'perfect', and legend says Camilla was a famous Roman warrior who ran so fast, she turned grass to ashes; a good moniker for fleet-footer pups like Greyhounds, Salukis and Whippets.

data.title: Candy
data.definition: Of course Candy can refer to all kinds of sweet treats, but in the Latin it also means 'dazzling white' or 'bright' - perfect for the sweetest of pups.

data.title: Captain Hook
data.definition: This name is from the famous pirate, who is the captain of the Jolly Roger, in Peter Pan. He has an iron hook on his severed hand, which is how he earnt the name.

data.title: Carla
data.definition: The feminine version of Karl, Charles or Carlos, the name Carla actually means 'free woman' and refers to strength.

data.title: Carly
data.definition: Carly is a shortened form of Carlotta or a feminine from the German 'Carl' and means 'free woman', as well as 'warrior' from the Old High German.

data.title: Carmela
data.definition: A beautiful and positive name, Carmela can mean 'golden' or 'garden', with the Latin meaning being a 'fruitful orchard'.

data.title: Carmen
data.definition: This girls name is of Hebrew and Latin origin, dervied from the word Karmel, Carmen means 'garden' or 'vineyard'. There is also a mountain mentioned of the same name in the Old Testament. Carmen has been famously used as the main character in George Bizet's opera 'Carmen'.

data.title: Casey
data.definition: The original meaning of the name Casey comes from the Latin to be 'vigilant', so it might be a good match for watchful dogs like the Bullmastiff, Rottweiler or Puli.

data.title: Casper
data.definition: Of course Casper makes us think of the friendly ghost, making this name perfect for a white dog. The name is actually of Scandinavian, and Persian origin, and it means 'bringer of treasure'; depending on what your pup brings you as a gift, you may agree to disagree!

data.title: Caspia
data.definition: Caspia is sometimes known as 'sea lavender', and comes from Mediterranean meadows where it clusters in little purple and white flowers. We think it makes an original name for nature-lovers and their pups!

data.title: Cassie
data.definition: Cassie is a short version of Cassandra meaning 'shining upon men' and famous in Greek mythology for being a prophetess, or fortune-teller. Cassie also comes from the the ancient Irish Cassidy, or those with curly hair; great for Poodles, Portguese Water Dogs and the Curly-Coated Retriever.

data.title: Chai
data.definition: Most famous as the spicy concoctions we all know and love, Chai also means 'alive' and in many cultures, just means 'tea'!

data.title: Chance
data.definition: Good fortune and luck shine down on Chance! A lovely name for an adoptee getting a second-chance at life.

data.title: Chanel
data.definition: The ancient French meaning of Chanel is 'pipe', but the name is undoubtedly better known for its high-fashion associations! Perfect for pampered pooches with impeccable taste.

data.title: Charlie
data.definition: A German name meaning 'free man'. Charlie is a popular name for many fictional characters, including Charlie Brown from Peanuts and Charlie Bucket in Charlie and the Chocolate Factory.

data.title: Charming
data.definition: We all want our prince charming, but sometimes they come on four legs, not two! The name Charming means 'delightful', and is famous as the name for Prince Charming, Cinderella's prince.

data.title: Chester
data.definition: Chester originally means 'fortress' or 'camp', giving it a strong, protective feeling!

data.title: Chewie
data.definition: Han Solo's Wookiee side-kick and pilot of the Millennium Falcon, Chewbacca—or Chewie, for short—is one of the series' most famous characters. Bringing home a Yorkie? We think Chewie is the perfect name because—you guessed it—Yorkies look like miniature Chewbaccas!

data.title: Chico
data.definition: Chico is a name of Spanish origin and literally means 'little boy'. This is a sweet pet name to give your small pup, because as we well know - although they may be small, they are almost always also mighty!

data.title: Chief
data.definition: Chief is a male name of Latin origin, which simply means 'leader'. If you have a male pup that rules the roost, or at least likes to think that he will, then this is a great name for them.

data.title: Chili
data.definition: We all associate chili with things which are spicy and hot; whether that be their firey temperament or the colour of their coat, this is a name that packs a bit of a punch!

data.title: Chiquita
data.definition: This is a Spanish name and means 'little girl'; a cute choice for smaller pups.

data.title: Chloe
data.definition: Chloe is a lovely Springlike name referring to new growth. In Greek mythology Chloe means 'young green shoot' and is an alternate name for the Goddess of fertility and agriculture.

data.title: Chopper
data.definition: The name chopper means 'a person who chops', and has historically been a surname in the US. This would be a great name for a pet that loves to chew on their toys a little too enthusiastically!

data.title: Chorizo
data.definition: If you haven't tried this spicy Spanish sausage, have you even lived? We love this name as a cheeky twist for long-backed dogs like dachshunds and corgis!

data.title: Chun
data.definition: A name of Chinese origin, Chun means ‘season of spring’. It may symbolise a new beginning for both you and your pup, full of hope and undoubtedly, some surprises!

data.title: Churro
data.definition: Churros are most famously known as the fried Spanish dessert dough, but did you know the name actually originated from a breed of sheep? We think this would make an unusual alternative for sheepdogs like Border Collies or Shetland Sheeodogs!

data.title: Cinnamon
data.definition: This warm, brown-coloured spice has its origins in Sri Lanka, and is known for its aromatic taste and golden colour. A fun, spunky name for firecrackers!

data.title: Clara
data.definition: A lovely name of Latin origin Clara means 'clear', 'bright', and 'famous'. All of us can admit that our pups bring a little brightness into our lives!

data.title: Cleo
data.definition: Cleo might be a short-form of the famous Egyptian Queen Cleopatra, and can be fittingly translated from the Greek to mean 'pride', 'fame' or 'glory'. One for the pups who hold their heads high!

data.title: Clifford
data.definition: Clifford the Big Red Dog is of course a childhood favorite - from both books and cartoons - and would suit big family-friendly breeds ready to be a part of the action!

data.title: Clio
data.definition: Clio was the ancient Greek muse of heroic poetry and history, and the name means 'glory'. A more unusual spelling and take on the more well-known 'Cleo'.

data.title: Clyde
data.definition: If you've got a Bonnie, you're going to need a Clyde - but even solo, this name of Scottish origin works perfectly, meaning 'warm' and 'friendly'. Clyde is a great name for a pup that is most obviously going to get themselves into mischief!

data.title: Coco
data.definition: This one's delicious, as it means 'chocolate bean'! Great for a little one with a cocoa-coloured coat, or of course, for pawrents who are fashion fans of the house of Chanel.

data.title: Cody
data.definition: Cody means 'sweet and helpful one' and is derived from Gaelic, making it a perfect name for pup's with impawtant jobs to do - from emotional support pets to guide dogs.

data.title: Comet
data.definition: Comet comes from the Greek and means 'head with long hair'; which brings to mind the celestial phenomenon with its blazing tail! If you've got a pup with a tail that never stops wagging, a fleet-of-foot breed prone to bursts of speed, or perhaps a long-haired beauty like the Afghan, this might be a great fit.

data.title: Contessa
data.definition: Have a royal canine in you house? This is an Italian female name, and means 'countess' or 'royalty'.

data.title: Cookie
data.definition: Of course we're all fans of this sweet treat, but did you know Cookie was also the nickname of the Queen's Mother?

data.title: Cooper
data.definition: Famous for the great MINI Cooper, this name has a lot more to it than a car brand! This is a name of British origin, and means 'barrel maker', originally given as a surname to a person who had that occupation.

data.title: Cosmo
data.definition: Cosmo, meaning beauty, order and organisation, is of Greek origin but let's be honest, we hear 'Cosmo' and think of the cocktail, don't we? If you're looking for a fun name for a lifetime of happy hours, this may be it.

data.title: Cricket
data.definition: You might think of the sport, or perhaps the loud chirrupy insect which is closer to the original meaning from the French, 'to creak' or 'to rattle'.

data.title: Crush
data.definition: A crush is that feeling of love and admiration for somebody. Crush is a great name for your pup because of your overwhelming love for them!

data.title: Daffy
data.definition: Meaning silly or strange often in a way that is funny, Daffy is a great name for the mischevious and playful dogs out there.

data.title: Daisy
data.definition:
The happiest of little flowers, Daisy means 'Day's Eye' because it opens its petals when the sun rises, and is as fresh, energetic and sunny as it's namesake suggests.

 

As the name of fashionable and fierce Daisy Duck, this is also a great name for any avid Disney fan (or owner of a sassy pup!)

data.title: Dakota
data.definition: Dakota is from the Native American meaning a 'friend' or an 'ally'; we couldn't think of a better name for man's best mate!

data.title: Dana
data.definition: This name has lots of meanings, from the Hebrew 'God is my judge' to the Arabic 'pearl'. Celtic mythology says Dana was a Goddess linked to generosity, wisdom and power.

data.title: Dante
data.definition: Dante is a Latin name meaning 'everlasting', and 'enduring', which is fitting given its most famous namesake wrote the classic 'The Divine Comedy' - an apt description of most days when you've got a new pup in your life!

data.title: Daphne
data.definition: Daphne was the Greek daughter of a River God, and her name means 'laurel tree'. One for water-lovers like Labradors, Irish Water Spaniels and Otterhounds.

data.title: Darla
data.definition: An Old English favourite meaning 'darling' or 'dearly beloved'.

data.title: Darlene
data.definition: An Old English name meaning 'darling' or 'dearly beloved'.

data.title: Darwin
data.definition: Of course we probably most associate the name with the famous English naturalist and father of evolution, Charles. However Darwin itself actually means ‘dear friend’, which is the perfect description for our pets.

data.title: Dax
data.definition: This is a French name that means 'leader'. This name is perfect for you pet, as it is short, and straight-forward.

data.title: Delia
data.definition: Britain's most famous Delia might be best known for her cookbooks, but the name originally came from Greece meaning 'born on the island of Delos'.

data.title: Delilah
data.definition: Delilah takes its meaning from the Hebrew for 'delicate', though you may know it better from the Tom Jones classic!

data.title: Destiny
data.definition: If having a pet has always been your fate, then Destiny might be the right choice for you! Sure to bring you good fortune and plenty of love.

data.title: Dexter
data.definition: You might associate Dexter with the 2006 TV series about a man with a double life, but the name itself is of Latin origin and actually means 'right-handed' or 'fortunate' - perfect for your right-hand man and partner in crime. The name can also symbolise skill, so would be fitting for those show dogs out there!

data.title: Diamond
data.definition: The most brilliant of gems, Diamond is a name perfectly suited to the brightest part of your day - your pooch!

data.title: Dibby
data.definition: Dibby is a girls name of African origin, which means 'a fortune-teller'.

data.title: Diego
data.definition: Diego is a male name of Spanish origin and means 'supplanter'; which is just about the experience of any dog owner who's let their pup into their bed, isn't it?

data.title: Diesel
data.definition: One of our more modern names, Diesel is a German name literally derived from the fuel, though it's also famous for being the surname of Vin Diesel from Fast and Furious. Either way; a great name for speedy dogs with a whole lot of energy!

data.title: Dino
data.definition: Dino is a boy's name of Italian origin and can mean either "little sword" or "brave bear" - of course it also makes us think of the short-form for "dinosaur" which carries with it a sense of strength and certainly, size!

data.title: Diva
data.definition: Did you know Diva is actually the Latin word for goddess? Whether you like this interpretation, or prefer the modern association to celebrated women, this is a strong name for a strong character for sure!

data.title: Dixie
data.definition: Dixie means 'tenth' and is of French origin, from the number 'ten' or 'dix'.

data.title: Dobby
data.definition: Dobby, known famously for the House Elf in Harry Potter, Dobby is a name of British origin, meaning ‘of Rob: Benevolent elf’. There are many dogs that look like dobby, and that isn’t a bad thing. Dobby was kind, caring and would do anything for his masters. If this sounds like your pup, then this is the perfect name for them.

data.title: Dodger
data.definition:
Dodger is a male name derived from the word 'to dodge' meaning 'a person who dodges things'. If you have a pup that manages to come out of things unscathed, or gets into mischeif but never gets caught, then this is a great name choice for them.

Famous namesakes include The Artful Dodger from Charles Dickens' novel Oliver Twist, and Dodger from Oliver & Company.

data.title: Dolly
data.definition: Hello, Dolly! This name actually means 'gift of God', and is perhaps most famously associated with songstress Dolly Parton.

data.title: Domino
data.definition: Domino actually comes from the Latin meaning 'Lord' or 'Master', but we think the spotty tile game of the same name lends itself better to breeds like Dalmatians, English Setters and Great Danes!

data.title: Doodle
data.definition: The name doodle is a British origin name, that means 'fool' and 'simpleton'. Doodle isn't one of the most common names, so your dog will certainly stand out.

data.title: Dory
data.definition: The fish that we all know and love from the classic film, Finding Nemo. Dory is a female name that means 'gift of God', although Dory seems rather aloof and unaware of her surroundings she is a genuine friend that helps him out no matter what!

data.title: Dot
data.definition: Dot is an Old English name meaning 'Gift of God', and we love it for our spotted friends too! Breeds like Dalmatians, Australian Shepherds and even spotty Terriers will suit this little name.

data.title: Dozer
data.definition: Do you have a pup that tends to sleep a LOT? This is the perfect name for them. Dozer is someone that likes to nap - so, if you have a pup you know will be likely to spend the majority of their time sleeping, pick this name for them, and it wont disappoint!

data.title: Draco
data.definition: Draco, known famously for the Slytherin rival of Harry Potter, actually derives from the Latin for ‘serpent’ or ‘dragon’. Might be back to the Sorting Hat if you're not happy with this one!

data.title: Dream
data.definition: This name doesn’t need much of a introduction. The name dream is a word that allows the mind to drift away and imagine some of the greatest things. It is an American word that means 'joyus music'. If your pup is a dream come true then we strongly suggest choosing this name for them!

data.title: Duchess
data.definition: Of Latin origin, Duchess derives from the word 'dux' and means 'leader' or 'commander'. If your dog rules the roost, you've got the right name for a lady with lots of attitude!

data.title: Duff
data.definition: Duff is a popular Scottish surname and means 'dark'. A short, snappy choice for black-haired beauties like the Flatcoated Retriever, Scottish Terrier or Scottish Deerhound.

data.title: Duke
data.definition: Duke is a name of Latin origin, meaning 'leader'. With a name like Duke your pup will be up to any task - even if it's just bringing in the post!

data.title: Dulce
data.definition: Coming from both the ancient Latin and the Spanish for 'sweet', (who can stop when they start on the Dulce de Leche?!), this is a lovely name for charming pups with a delightful disposition.

data.title: Dylan
data.definition: This is a male name of Welsh origin and means 'son of sea'. This is a great name for a pup that loves a good splash around in anything from a puddle to a lake, like Labradors, Water Spaniels or the Portuguese Water Dog.

data.title: Earl
data.definition: Earl is a boys name of Old English origin. Earl means 'nobleman', 'warrior', and 'prince'. The name Earl is derived from the British title of a person, from the old English word eorl.

data.title: Echo
data.definition: The story of Echo is a sad one, as she was a magical wood nymph whose unrequited love for Narcisses eventually caused her to fade to nothing except the shadows of her voice. Don't let the tragic history put you off what is a pretty-sounding name and fantastic for dogs whose job is to listen, like shepherding breeds Rough Collie, Shetland Sheepdog and German Shepherd.

data.title: Eden
data.definition: Eden is known for being the most famous biblical garden in the world, and literally means 'place of pleasure' in the original Hebrew.

data.title: Edie
data.definition: A shortened form of 'Edith', this name comes from the Old English meaning 'prosperous in war'.

data.title: Ella
data.definition: Ella can mean 'she' or 'her' in Spanish, or taken as a short form of Eleanor or Ellen it can also mean 'light'. In modern Hebrew it also means 'goddess'; a feminine, ethereal name all around!

data.title: Ellie
data.definition: Ellie means 'shining light' and is of Greek origin.

data.title: Elmer
data.definition: Elmer is a male name of British origin, meaning 'noble' and 'famous'. We know that our pups like to be the centre of attention no matter what, so if you know yours will be drawn to the lime light, this is the name for them!

data.title: Elsa
data.definition: Elsa means 'pledged to God' in German, and is better known as the iconic ice queen heroine in Disney's Frozen. A good choice for snow-loving dogs like Huskys, Greater Swiss Mountain Dogs and Alaskan Malamutes - the cold never bothered them anyway!

data.title: Emma
data.definition: Emma means 'universal', and it's certainly a popular name! Whether it makes you think of the classic Jane Austen heroine, 'Baby Spice' Bunton or actresses Stone, Thompson or Watson, it's loved far and wide!

data.title: Emmy
data.definition: An affectionate form of Emma, Emmy also means 'universal' and is perhaps most associated with the glittering award show!

data.title: Eric
data.definition: A masculine name of Old Norse origin meaning 'forever ruler'.  As the name of the dashing human in The Little Mermaid who Ariel falls for, this name is fit for a little Prince Eric!

data.title: Esmeralda
data.definition: This is a female name of spanish origin. The name means 'emerald'. The heroine in the Disney film The Hunchback of Notre-Dame, is called Esmeralda in reference to the jewel she wears around her neck.

data.title: Espresso
data.definition: We all need our dose of caffeine, and what better way to do it than with a powerful shot that packs a punch. Espresso is an energetic and joyful name for any pooch, big or small, that brings that extra surge of energy into your life!

data.title: Eva
data.definition: A variation on the biblical name Eve, the Hebrew origin means 'life'.

data.title: Evan
data.definition: Evan is a strong masculine male name of Welsh origin, that means 'lord is gracious'. In Hebrew the word Evan means 'rock', and in Greek it means 'good messenger'. This is a great name for you pup who is not only your friend, but protector too.

data.title: Faegan
data.definition: Faegan actually menas 'joyful', which is what dog-ownership is all about! It's also the name of the wizard from “The Chronicles of Blood and Stone”.

data.title: Faith
data.definition: English Puritans popularised this name in the 17th Century, meaning 'to trust' in the original Latin and associated with confidence and belief. A great name for a loyal companion and working sidekicks like Border Collies, German Shepherds and Shelties.

data.title: Fang
data.definition: A no-brainer if a Neopolitan Mastiff is your pup of choice - made most famous by Hagrid’s gargantuan pet, Fang also refers to a large sharp tooth - beared always as a grin, we hope!

data.title: Fauna
data.definition: Fauna is such a sweet name, taken from the Roman goddess of animals and literally meaning 'young deer'. She's also one of the three fairies in Disney's Sleeping Beauty.

data.title: Felix
data.definition: Now of course, the most famous Felix WE know is our very own mischievious feline! However, it can absolutely also work for your pup - the name means 'happy' and 'fortune' - so whether they're always 'up to mischief' or just have a perpetually sunny disposition, this is a great name for them.

data.title: Fergus
data.definition: Meaning the 'strong one' or 'masculine one', Fergus is a noble Scottish name which might suit bigger breeds like the Scottish Deerhound, Irish Wolfhound or Greyhound.

data.title: Fern
data.definition: Taken from the shade-loving plant, Fern has a vintage botanical feel perfect for gentle pets with a laid back attitude.

data.title: Fifi
data.definition: This is a perfect name for any fancy pooch and though officially the French name means 'Jehovah increases', it's become a short version of 'Josephine' and is generally thought of as a fun, flirty French name - perhaps perfect for any poodles coming into your life!

data.title: Fig
data.definition: A name of German origin, and a lovely symbol of growth and prosperity, Fig is a sweet little name for your new girl pup.

data.title: Finlay
data.definition: A classic Gaelic name, Finlay means 'fair-haired hero'. It might make a good choice for calm and confident emotional support breeds like the Labrador, Corgi or Cavalier King Charles Spaniel.

data.title: Finley
data.definition: A male name with Irish origins, Finley means 'fair-haired'. This is a perfect name for those pups that have a lighter coat colour.

data.title: Finn
data.definition: Finn may be associated with the surname from the classic 'Adventures of Huckleberry Finn' by Mark Twain, but this name has a little more history behind it. The name Finn is originally of Irish decent, and was name of an Irish mythical warrior in folk law history, known to be courageous, intelligent, and brave. We think that Finn is the perfect name for those protectors in your life, whether they are little, or large.

data.title: Fiona
data.definition: Fiona is originally a Scottish name, meaning 'white' or 'fair', and was apparently dreamt up by a poet in the 19th century. She's also the Princess wife of the lovable ogre in Shrek.

data.title: Fisher
data.definition: Unsurprisingly, the name Fisher was primarily used as an occupational surname, meaning 'fisherman'. That being said, it's the perfect choice for water-loving pups, whether they actually catch anything, or not!

data.title: Flash
data.definition: Does your pup run past you or have their mad 5 minutes at the speed of light? Well we have just the name for you. Flash is of American origin and means 'bright light', famous for the comic character Flash Gordon.

data.title: Flit
data.definition: Flit is a girls name that means 'move swiftly and lightly'. This would be a perfect name choice for a pup that is quick on their feet, much like the hummingbird Flit in Disney's Pocahontas!

data.title: Flora
data.definition: Flora is a floral name meaning 'Flower' of Scottish and Latin origin, best known for the name of the Roman goddess of flowers and spring. Made popular by one of the three fairies in Disney's Sleeping Beauty.

data.title: Flounder
data.definition: Flounder would be a great name for a clumsy pup, with Flounder meaning someone who struggles to stay upright or feels great confusion. The name flounder is also the name of Ariel's fishy companion in Disney's The Little Mermaid.

data.title: Flynn
data.definition:
Flynn is a male name of Irish origin meaning "son of the red-haired one". If you haven;t guessed it already, this would make a great name for those ginger pups!

One of the most famous Flynn's is Flynn Rider, the swashbuckling rogue from Disney's Tangled, so this name would also suit a dog with a hint of mischief in their bones.

data.title: Foxy
data.definition: A fun, feisty name from their wild namesake friend, this might be a good match for the Spitz, Shiba Inu or Chihuahua breeds with their cheeky faces and auburn coats!

data.title: Freckles
data.definition: Does your dog have cute little spots, not so much like a dalmation, but less defined, like a splat of paint? Freckles is the perfect name for them!

data.title: Fred
data.definition: The English origin of Fred means 'peaceful ruler' and can also mean 'sage' or 'wise'. A classic human name for your companion.

data.title: Freesia
data.definition: Freesias are sweet-smelling flowers, and the name might make a good alternative to more common florals like Lily or Rose.

data.title: Frida
data.definition: Frida is a German name that means ‘beautiful’, and is often associated with the artist Frida Kahlo and her vivid self-portraits! A lovely name for a pup who's beautiful inside and out.

data.title: Fritz
data.definition: Fritz is a German name meaning 'peaceful ruler', so possibly not one for the barkers, yappers and growlers in your life!

data.title: Frode
data.definition: Frode in the old Norse means ‘wise one’; not to be confused with the famous Hobbit Frodo Baggins! They might not always seem too smart whilst you're fishing a tennis ball out from under a table for the millionth time, but we swear they have a plan..!

data.title: Frollo
data.definition: Frollo is a male name of Latin origin. The meaning of the name is 'killed by arthur' and is part of the Arthurian Legend. Famously used as the surname for the Archdeacon of Notre Dame Cathedral, in The Hunchback of Notre Dame, by Victor Hugo, as well as the villain in the Disney movie of the same name.

data.title: Gabby
data.definition: The shortened female form of Gabriel, Gabby means 'God is my strength'.

data.title: Gadget
data.definition: The name "Gadget" is a playful and whimsical name that typically refers to a mechanical or electronic device with a specific purpose or function. The name suggests a canine companion who is curious, inventive, and brings a sense of cleverness and resourcefulness to everything they do!

data.title: Gaia
data.definition: Gaia was the original mother goddess; goddess of the earth, and the name means 'rejoice' in Greek.

data.title: Gaston
data.definition: Gaston is a boy's name of French origin. Famously known for the macho villian of Beauty and the Beast, or the author of the Phatom of the Opera, Gaston Leroux. The name Gaston is a classic name in France, and means 'the foreigner' or 'the guest'.

data.title: Gemma
data.definition: From the Italian meaning 'precious stone', Gemma is literally a jewel of a name for the most precious pup in your life!

data.title: Genie
data.definition:
At some point in our lives we have wanted to summon a Genie to help us fulfil our wishes. But do you know what the name means?

Genie is a name is of Greek and Hebrew origin, and it means 'well-born' or 'God is gracious'.

data.title: George
data.definition: George is a solid, trusty name originally meaning 'earthworker' which we think makes it perfect for dogs who just love to dig, scratch and paw at the ground - terriers, we're looking at you!

data.title: Georgia
data.definition: Georgia is actually an English name originally meaning 'farmer'! Georgia is also the name of a US State, famous for its juicy peaches!

data.title: Gia
data.definition: A shorter form of Gianna, this Italian name means 'God's gracious gift'.

data.title: Gidget
data.definition: Gidget means 'small girl' and has the mischievious energy to match toy breeds like Affenpinschers, Pekingese and Papillons.

data.title: Gigi
data.definition: An easy to call name for spunky girls, Gigi has a French feeling to it.

data.title: Ginger
data.definition: Ginger might be the ultimate Christmassy flavour, but it's also a vibrant red flower. The perfect pick for pups with a bit of a spicy side.

data.title: Ginny
data.definition: Was Ginny Weasley anyone else's favourite in Harry Potter? The English name that we all love, is a shortened form of 'Virginia', and is perfect for your pure-hearted pup!

data.title: Gisli
data.definition: The Old Norse and Scandinavian meaning of Gisli is 'ray of sunshine'; something you'll need to bear in mind when your golden labradors and retrievers are having the time of their lives in muddy rivers!

data.title: Gizmo
data.definition: Anyone else have a pup that can go from absolute angel to gremlin in a matter of minutes? Gizmo is a great name for them, as it's the character in cult movie 'Gremlins', and also means 'one who is playful'. They don't mean it, really...!

data.title: Glen
data.definition: Named after a Scottish word for 'valley', Glen would be a good match for composed, gentle dogs who love to be outdoors.

data.title: Glinda
data.definition: The beautiful and kind good witch of Oz gets her name from the meaning 'fair', which suits her, and any pup with a sweet nature and pretty face, just as well!

data.title: Goldie
data.definition: Unsurprisingly, this name means 'made of gold' and is a popular option for lush gold-coated Golden Retrievers, Cocker Spaniels and Goldendoodles.

data.title: Goofy
data.definition: Goofy is Mickey Mouse's best friend, known for his kind heart, fumbling ways and hilarious personality. Great for a dog that's larger than life, a little clumsy and lovable!

data.title: Goose
data.definition: Silly goose - a bit of a comical name as of course, it refers to the bird who at times, can be a bit unpredictable!

data.title: Gothel
data.definition: Mother knows best! Famous as the evil Mother Gothel in the Disney film Tangled, Gothel is a name of German origin which means 'nurse' or 'foster mother'.

data.title: Grace
data.definition: The Latin origin of this name means 'charm', whilst the Greeks associated it with beauty and joy. Some also say it's connected to a blessing.

data.title: Gracie
data.definition: A sweet nickname derived from Grace, Gracie is a 'favour' or a 'blessing' with plenty of charm.

data.title: Greta
data.definition: Greta is a classic German name which means 'pearl' and evokes the glamorous days of Hollywood icon Greta Garbo. A classy name for German breeds like the Dachshund, Weimeraner or Doberman.

data.title: Gretchen
data.definition: The German origin of this name is 'little pearl' and it's traditional origins might conjure up images of dirndls and Alpine meadows.

data.title: Grizzly
data.definition: The name "Grizzly" is often associated with the grizzly bear, a large and powerful species of bear found in North America. It can also mean 'grey-haired', so might make a good name for breeds of that colour, or just those older pups getting a little 'grizzly' in the muzzle!

data.title: Gus
data.definition: Most will recognise this name from the Disney film Cinderella, as the little mouse Gus Gus. Gus actually means 'majestic' and 'exalted' which just goes to show, amazing things can come in tiny packages!

data.title: Hades
data.definition: Hades is the god of the underworld, but you Greek mythology lovers already knew that! He has connections with Hercules, and is an essential character that has inspired many musical, art works, and books. The name means 'unseen', but ironically Hades is one of the most celebrated of the Greek gods.

data.title: Hagrid
data.definition: Fans of Harry Potter will know Hagrid as the forest-dwelling giant and gamekeeper of Hogwarts, making this a great name for big breeds that love to be out in the wild, and those with strong protective tendencies.

data.title: Hai
data.definition: This is a beautiful name option for any pet. Hai, from Chinese origin, means ‘ocean’, so would suit any pet from cool, calm and collected to those apt to a bit of stormy behaviour!

data.title: Hailey
data.definition: In Norse Hailey means 'hero', and in Irish 'wise one', whilst the English refer to it as a hay field or remote valley, but the name is probably most connected to Halley's Comet which circles the sun every 75 years.

data.title: Halley
data.definition: Edmond Halley was an English astronomer who first discovered Halley's Comet which is what usually comes to mind when you hear this name. However it's also a name meaning 'heroine', originating from Scandinavia. Either way, we think it makes an unusual name for those strong-willed or fast-footed dogs!

data.title: Halo
data.definition: Our pets are our little angels, despite the trouble they may get themselves into from time to time. The name Halo is an English word that has been used since the 16th century. Halo means 'divine light'. For the angels in your life, this name is the perfect match.

data.title: Hamish
data.definition: Hamish is one of the most Scottish-sounding names out there, and means 'supplanter' - a concept all dog-owners who've been kicked off their own sofa by their furry friend will be familiar with!

data.title: Hannah
data.definition: Hannah comes from the Hebrew name Channah, which means 'favour' or 'grace'.

data.title: Happy
data.definition: This name doesn’t need much of a defintion. That feeling you get when you watch your pet go crazy round and round in circles? Or when they snuggle down next to you, that’s the feeling of Happiness. This is a great name for your pup.

data.title: Harley
data.definition: You might immediately think of the growling Harley Davidson bikers, or villanous Harley Quinn from Batman, but the name's origin is actually from the much softer Old English for 'long field'.

data.title: Harper
data.definition: Is there anything more romantic than the soft strains of a harp? Harper literally means 'someone who plays the harp' and whilst that might be a stretch too far for even the most talented pooch, it makes a lovely musical name.

data.title: Harris
data.definition: Meaning 'son of Harry', Harris is also the name of a Scottish island known for sandy beaches and rugged mountains and makes a classic name for dogs with a quiet confidence.

data.title: Hazel
data.definition: Directly named after the Hazel tree, the name is inspired by the little tree which drops hazelnuts in the late summer.

data.title: Heidi
data.definition: A German name originally meaning 'noble', the most famous Heidi is of course the Alpine-dwelling girl of the Swiss children's book.

data.title: Heinz
data.definition: Heinz is a name of German origin, meaning 'ruler of the home'. However it's probably most famously known as the brand behind some of our store cupboard favourites, and the phrase 'Heinz 57 varieties' is often attributed to mixed-breed dogs and even Jack Russell Terriers, thanks to their combination of many breeds!

data.title: Helios
data.definition: A Greek name meaning 'sun', Helios was the sun god, whose sister Selene was the moon goddess. A celestial choice for sunshiney personalities or golden-haired pets!

data.title: Henry
data.definition: Henry, is one of the more common French male names. It is derived from the French name Henri, meaning 'house ruler'. Great for a pup that rules your household! The name has also had many famous owners throughout history, from our own King Henry, (well 8 to be exact!) and the formidable batman actor, Henry Cavil.

data.title: Hera
data.definition: Hera was the Greek goddess of the hearth and as the wife of Zeus, the Queen to all the Mount Olympus gods. Unsurprisingly then, the name Hera also means warrior, or queen, and is a strong name for a single-minded girl who likes to rule the roost.

data.title: Hercules
data.definition: Hercules was a Roman demigod known for his incredible strength! A powerful name for bigger breeds like the Great Dane, English Mastiff and Saint Bernard.

data.title: Hero
data.definition: The name "Hero" is derived from the Greek word "heros," which refers to a person of great courage and strength who performs noble and heroic acts. The name embodies bravery, loyalty, and a strong sense of protection. It would be a fitting choice for a dog owner seeking a name that reflects their furry friend's courageous and devoted personality.

data.title: Hershey
data.definition: The American chocolate brand is best known for its 'kisses', so this is a sweet pick for that affectionate pup in your life.

data.title: Holly
data.definition: A Christmassy name connected to the evergreen Holly trees with their festive red berries, this might be a good match for December-born pets.

data.title: Honey
data.definition: Winnie-the-Pooh's favourite snack is this sweet sticky nectar, a nice pick for golden-hued breeds like Labradors, Golden Retrievers and Chow Chows.

data.title: Hope
data.definition: A pure and elegant name, and one of the three virtues along with Faith and Charity, Hope might be a fitting name for a loving service dog, or an adoptee getting a second chance.

data.title: Hoss
data.definition: The name Hoss is a male name of Swedish origin meaning 'strong and friendly', making it a great name for those gentle giants.

data.title: Houdini
data.definition: The perfect name for the escape-artists of the pack! Made famous by the father of all magicians, this would suit a free-spirited, determined-natured pup with a cheeky streak that never fails to surprise!

data.title: Huan
data.definition: The name Huan comes from Chinese origins and means ‘happiness’, ‘fortune’ and ‘pleased’. If you have a pet that seems to never stop wagging that lovely tail, then this is the perfect name for them!

data.title: Huang
data.definition: Are you struggling to find a suitable name for a golden pup that is a bit different to the rest? Why not try Huang, of Mandarin origin and meaning ‘yellow’.

data.title: Hugo
data.definition: This name of German origin means 'intellect' or 'mind', so might suit one of the brainiest breeds out there like the Border Collie, German Shepherd or Poodle.

data.title: Hunter
data.definition: There's no great mystery behind the meaning of this name; which literally means 'man who hunts'! If you've got a fantastic working dog that's helping you retrieve, this might be a great fit for them.

data.title: Ibby
data.definition: Sometimes used as a shortened version of Elisabeth, the name means 'God is my oath'.

data.title: Idefix
data.definition: This is an interesting one, as derived from the French, the name comes from the phrase 'idee fixe', - to be 'headstrong', making it a great name for those dogs absolutely fixated on their favourite thing; be it a ball, their dinner, or that squirrel that always gets away! It's also the name of the dog in the famous cartoons 'Asterix'.

data.title: Iggy
data.definition: This is a sparky name for your pet, meaning 'firey one', and is a great option to give to your sassy pup!

data.title: Indy
data.definition: Indy unsurprisingly is short for 'independent'. If you have a pet that likes cuddles, but on their terms, and can be a bit of a lone wolf, then this name will suit them perfectly.

data.title: Inez
data.definition: The name Inez originally comes from Spain and means 'pure' or 'holy'.

data.title: Ingrid
data.definition: A name of Norse origin, Ingrid means 'fair' and 'beautiful'. In Scandinavia Ingrid is still considered a rather royal name.

data.title: Iona
data.definition: Iona is the name of another picturesque Scottish island, and is quite a traditional name linked to natural forces like wind, water and the sun. In Greek it is also associated with 'dove', making it a pretty, peaceful name.

data.title: Iris
data.definition: Best known as the beautiful Spring blossom, Iris comes from the Greek meaning 'rainbow' and was also the Goddess of the same who road her colourful bridge between heaven and earth. This connection to colour is also how the pigmented part of the eye came to be named!

data.title: Isabel
data.definition: Isabel is an angleic name for a girl, it is the Spanish variation of Elizabeth, and means 'pledge to God'. The name was first dervied from Elizabeth in the Middle Ages, as was origianlly written as Elisabel.

data.title: Islay
data.definition: Literally meaning 'island' in Scottish, Islay is also the name of the Hebridian island most famous for its whiskys; dark, beautiful and totally enchanting.

data.title: Ivy
data.definition: A simple, charming name which comes from the elegant trailing plant and originally means 'vine'.

data.title: Izzy
data.definition: Sometimes used as a shortened version of Elisabeth, the name means 'God is my oath'.

data.title: Jack
data.definition: Jack is a classic name, and means 'god is gracious'.

data.title: Jada
data.definition: In Arabic the name means 'goodness', but it's more closely associated to the gemstone Jade with Jada being a popular Spanish version.

data.title: Jade
data.definition: A cool green stone meant to represent wisdom, justice and courage, Jade is a meant to promote joyfulness and love.

data.title: Jafar
data.definition: Jafar is a male name of Arabic origin that means Stream. The most famous Jafar is the villain in Disney's Aladdin.

data.title: Jagger
data.definition: Does your pet have moves like Jagger? Less excitingly perhaps, the name Jagger means 'carter' and 'peddlar', but we prefer the connotations to lead singer of the Rolling Stones, Mick Jagger, and the subsequent song referring to his fancy footwork!

data.title: Jake
data.definition: Jake is boy's name of Hebrew origin meaning 'supplanter' and let's fact it, if you're a dog owner who's let their pup onto the bed, you'll know exactly how that feels!

data.title: Jane
data.definition: Jane is a common name for girls, it is a British origin name means 'god is gracious', and has been coined for phrases such as 'Jane Doe' and 'Plain Jane'. Jane first became popular in the mid-16th century where it was used as an alternative to the name Joan.

data.title: Jaq
data.definition: Jaq is a male name of French origin and means 'God is gracious'. Jaq is an alternate form of the name Jack. The characteristics associated with this name are intelligence, and companionship - perfect for a pup.

data.title: Jasmine
data.definition: Jasmine is a beautifully fragrant white flower, and comes from the Persian which also means 'gift from God'. Jasmine is also the fiercely independent Princess in Disney's Aladdin.

data.title: Jasper
data.definition: Jasper can variously mean 'treasurer' or 'treasure-bearer' and is a great name for pups who can't help but bring back wonderous treasures to present you with; just make sure you show your appreciation before delicately disposing!

data.title: Jax
data.definition: If you're looking for a bit of a twist on classics like Jack or Jaques, this might be the right one for you - meaning 'son of Jack', X marks the spot with this spunky little name!

data.title: Jenna
data.definition: Jenna means 'fair' and has been variously connected to magical beings, phantoms, waves and shadows.

data.title: Jersey
data.definition: Jersey is one of the Channel Islands floating off the coast of England, and the name literally means 'grassy island'.

data.title: Jet
data.definition: Swift, sleek and dark as night, Jet is a powerful name for athletic black breeds like Flatcoated Retrievers, Rottweilers or Groenendaels.

data.title: Jewel
data.definition: No great surprises here; the name Jewel literally means 'precious stone'. Your pup will be sure to know their worth with this special name.

data.title: Jiminy
data.definition: Thought to be derived from the name James, Jiminy is of Hebrew origin, and means 'supplanter'. With Jiminy Cricket being Pinocchio's conscience in Disney's Pinocchio, Jiminy is a brilliant name for a pup who guides you through life.

data.title: Jimmy
data.definition: A boys name of Hebrew origin that means 'he who supplants'. If your pup likes to push their way into situations, then this is a great name choice.

data.title: Jin
data.definition: Do you have a gold or yellow-coloured dog, and are looking for an alternative and obscure name for them? We have just the name for you! Jin, which is traditionally used in Chinese culture for surnames, means ‘gold’ - great for Golden Retrievers, Shar-Peis, or Goldendoodles. You can find more yellow dogs here.

data.title: Jinx
data.definition: The word means a 'spell' or a 'charm' and is a fun little name - unless of course - you're the superstitious type!

data.title: Jolene
data.definition: Jolene, please don’t take him just because you can! We know, a great song, but a great name too.

data.title: Jolly
data.definition: This name has a mixture of origins, English, Scottish, and French. Jolly is primarily used as a nickname for someone that is cheerful, or attractive, and dervies from the Middle English word jolif joli meaning 'cherrful, lively, pretty'. The name has since become and name in its own right, rather than just being used as a nickname.

data.title: Josie
data.definition: Whilst this name might make you think of the cartoon pop band Josie and the Pussycats, it has an Old English origin meaning 'God increases'. A cute, friendly nickname for kittens and puppies alike!

data.title: Jovie
data.definition:
From the english word 'jovial'.

Jovie, no not like Bon Jovi, but the American rooted name, meaning ‘joyful’. The name is primarily used as a female name, which symbolises, happiness, greatness and kindness. If you have a pup that encompasses all of, or some of, these characteristics, this is a great name choice.

data.title: Joy
data.definition: Joy, one of the happiest names on this names list, is a female name of Latin origin that reall does mean 'happiness'. This is a great name to represent the little bundle of joy in your life.

data.title: Juan
data.definition: Juan is a Spanish origin name, meaning 'God is gracious', it is one of the most popular male names in Spanish culture and is the Spanish counterpart to the British name, John.

data.title: Judge
data.definition: Order, Order! Judge is a male name, of English origin that means 'decision maker'. Dogs tend to make their own decisions about sleep, play and more sleep. If yours is a decisive pup, even if it's just about exactly when it's time for dinner, then this is a great name choice.

data.title: Juju
data.definition: Juju is a girls name that is short form of Julia, Juliette and June, but more interestingly, is used as slang for a kind of magic or mystical property - if your dog has good juju, and let's face it, which ones don't, then this might be a great name for them!

data.title: Julia
data.definition: A very important name back in Roman times, Julia referred to woman living in the house of Julius Caesar, and also has connections to Jupiter meaning 'sky father'.

data.title: Juliet
data.definition: One of Shakespeare's starcrossed lovers, Juliet is a romantic name for sweet-natured pups, also meaning 'youthful'.

data.title: June
data.definition: Another name for you summer lovers. June is a gorgeous female name of Latin origin that means 'young'. The name comes from the summer month, and is derived from the name Juno, who was a powerful Roman goddess who was the protector of marriage and birth.

data.title: Juno
data.definition: Juno means 'youth' when taken from the original Latin, but she was also an ancient Roman Goddess. In fact, she was the most important one, the Queen of Heaven and Queen of all Gods, whose task was to protect the women of Rome. The month June is named after her; one for the Summer puppies!

data.title: Jupiter
data.definition: Jupiter is not just the name of the giant planet, but also the king of the Roman Gods. So, if you have a pup that you think shows themselves as a natural born leader, then this is the perfect name for them.

data.title: Jura
data.definition: One of the most untamed of the Scottish islands, Jura is the perfect name for charming girls with a wild side who love to run free.

data.title: Kaa
data.definition: The name Kaa is of Indian, Tamil, and Telugu origins and means 'joy'. It's most famously used in the MowgliI stories by Rudyard Kipling, as well as Disney's The Jungle Book.

data.title: Kai
data.definition: The name Kai has lots of associations; we like the Hawaiian meaning which is 'the sea'. An unusual choice for water-loving dogs who just can't stay dry!

data.title: Kairo
data.definition: The name Kairo is primarily a male name of Arabic origin that means 'victorious one'. The name is also famous as the place name in Egypt.

data.title: Kaiser
data.definition: Kaiser, a name of German origin meaning 'leader'. Famously known as the name for the band the Kaiser Chiefs, this name is sure to make any pup feel on top of the world!

data.title: Kallie
data.definition: Mirror mirror on the wall, who is the fairest of them all? Well, it might just be Kallie. The name means ‘fairest’ or ‘most beautiful’ and is a lovely name for a girl that really does steal all of the attention in the room.

data.title: Kari
data.definition: The Scandinavian God of Wind was named Kari, and it's associated with the meaning 'pure'. Whether your pup is more gentle breeze or tornado, this could be a cool name for their particular type of energy!

data.title: Karma
data.definition: This is a lovely name meaning ‘fate’ and ‘destiny’. We'd all be so lucky as to be reincarnated as a dog; one for those who have been very, very good!

data.title: Katniss
data.definition: The Hunger Games' heroine Katniss is known for her fighting spirit, leadership and bravery. Her name is inspired by a plant known as 'arrowhead'.

data.title: Kelda
data.definition: The Norse word for 'fountain', this name conjurs up the same kind of sparkling fresh energy as their namesakes!

data.title: Kelsey
data.definition: Kelsey is derived from Irish origin and means ‘brave’ and can represent strength and perseverance. If you think this sounds like your pup, then this is the perfect name for them.

data.title: Khloe
data.definition: Khloe, is a female name of Greek origin, which means ‘green shoot’ and ‘blossoming’. The name Khloe was also a title used for the Greek goddess of agriculture and the harvest. This name would be a great name to symbolise the blossoming of a new relationship with your pup.

data.title: Kiki
data.definition: Kiki means ‘double happiness’ and we couldn't think of a better way to describe dog ownership - chewed table legs aside!!

data.title: Kikka
data.definition: Quite a formidable name for those dogs who know they're the boss; in German Kikka means 'mistress of all', so if you've got a hard-headed hound on your hands who's head of the park, this could be perfect!

data.title: Kim
data.definition: Kim, the name of Britis origin is the short version of Kimberly. The name means 'god'. The name is most famous for being the name of Kim Kardashian.

data.title: Kira
data.definition: Kira can mean either 'dark-haired' or ‘strong woman’. Do you own a brave and determined pup, or one that has lovely dark fur? This might be the name for them.

data.title: Kit
data.definition: Kit is a short, snappy name which is always a good choice to help dogs understand they're being called! Of course, if you're getting two at the same time, we can also highly recommend 'Kat' to make a delicious duo!

data.title: Kitty
data.definition: Kitty is a female name, and although it may seem controversial to use such a name for a dog, hang in there! This name is of English origin and means 'pure'. This name has a lovely meaning and is a popular choice, the name Kitty was used for one of the Bennet sisters in Pride and Prejudice.

data.title: Kiwi
data.definition: Most famous as the national symbol of New Zealand, this name of course refers to both the native flightless bird, but also, the fuzzy little fruit!

data.title: Koda
data.definition: Meaning 'friend' in Japanese, what better name to give your new best friend than one that literally means friend?!

data.title: Kodiak
