data.definition: The name "Kodiak" is derived from the Kodiak Island in Alaska, which is known for its majestic landscapes and its population of Kodiak bears, the largest subspecies of brown bears. A good one for big bears like the Alaskan Malamute!

data.title: Kristoff
data.definition: The male name Kristoff is primarily of Scandinavian origin that means Christ-bearer. Most famous as the ice harvester Kristoff from Disney's smash hit film Frozen. This is a great name to highlight kindness and bravery in your pup.

data.title: Kronk
data.definition: Kronk was first founded in Holland, as a surname. The name became noted for its many branches in the region. It is also suggested that the name comes from the German word, Kranc, for an unwell person. Most famously, Kronk is Yzma's loyal assistant in Disney's Emperors New Groove.

data.title: Kyle
data.definition: Kyle is a male name of Scottish and Irish origin that means 'coal' . A fantastic name choice for a black or grey pup.

data.title: Lacey
data.definition: Surprisingly this name has nothing to do with the frilly material, rather it comes from the French region of 'Lassy' in Normandy! It still makes us think of all things delicate and dainty, so might be a good match for a little princess pooch too.

data.title: Lacy
data.definition: Surprisingly this name has nothing to do with the frilly material, rather it comes from the French region of 'Lassy' in Normandy! It still makes us think of all things delicate and dainty, so might be a good match for a little princess pooch too.

data.title: Lady
data.definition: The name "Lady" is an English term that refers to a woman of high social standing or nobility, and historically has meant 'head of the household'. So if you're ready to relinquish control to a little Lady, this might be the name for you!

data.title: Laika
data.definition: The story of Laika is a sad one; famously known as the first living being to orbit the earth via the Russian space programme, she was a stray dog whose name literally means "the barker". If you've got a yapper on your hands, or just think your dog is out of this world, Laika might be a good fit!

data.title: Lassie
data.definition: Perhaps one of the most famous fictional dogs, Lassie is best known as the super smart collie from TV and film who rescues lost children. The Rough Collie is a Scottish breed, and the name Lassie comes from the same place, meaning 'little girl'.

data.title: Layla
data.definition: A name of ancient Arabic origin, the name Layla has many meanings. However, the most common understanding of this name is ‘night’, or ‘dark’. So, if you are the owner of a black or grey pup, this would be a great name option.

data.title: Lea
data.definition: The female name Lea is of Hebrew origin and means 'delicate' or 'weary'. If you have a pup that is on the more cautious side of life, this is a great name for them.

data.title: Leela
data.definition: This is a great name for a playful pup. The name Leela can be loosely translated from the Sanskrit to mean 'play'. It is said to be variation of Laila and Lila, but is a playful and fun name in its own right.

data.title: Leia
data.definition: Probably best known as the name of the Star Wars Princess, Leia actually comes from Hebrew origins and means 'tired' - hardly what one would associate with one of the Rebel Alliance's great and fearless leaders! If you've got a spunky pup that springs into action (possiby from a supine position!!), this is a great name from one of the galaxy's greatest heroes!

data.title: Lena
data.definition: A name connected to many cultures including Persian, Hindi, Swedish and French, the name Lena has many meanings. However, the most common understanding of this name is ‘light’, ‘bright’ or ‘shining’. So, if you are the owner of a golden or white pup, this would be a great name option.

data.title: Lenny
data.definition: The name Lenny means 'brave as a lion', making it a great match for those pups who see themselves as your greatest protector; no matter their actual size!

data.title: Leo
data.definition: Leo is a male name that dates back centuries. The name Leo stems from the Latin word for 'lion'. Short form of Leonardo, the name Leo is often used for small breeds, to symbolise their big personalities! Famous, of course, for being the given name of Leonardo DiCaprio.

data.title: Leroy
data.definition: Leroy is a male name of French origin that means 'king'. This is a strong name that became popular in the mid 19th century.

data.title: Levi
data.definition: Levi means 'joined' or 'united' so if you and your pup are a match made in heaven, this might be a great choice to symbolise your coming together!

data.title: Lexi
data.definition: Lexi is derived from the name Alexandra and Alexis and the most common understanding of this name is ‘man’s defender’. If your pup is perhaps a little on the protective side, or just wants to make sure you (or their favourite toy!) are safe and sound, this might make a cute name for them.

data.title: Li Shang
data.definition: Li Shang means 'plum' and is a male surname famous for the combination of two given names of Mulan's mother and father.

data.title: Lian
data.definition: This is another lovely floral name with Chinese origins. Lian means, ‘graceful willow’ and ‘lotus flower’. The lotus flower is often used to symbolise rebirth and purity thanks to their ability to flourish and grow in the most unlikely environments. If you have a new pup, or have re-homed one, then this is the perfect name to symbolise new beginnings.

data.title: Libby
data.definition: A name of British origin, the name Libby is derived from the name Elizabeth. The most common understanding of this name is ‘God's promise’ and is of course most famously known as the name for our longest reigning monarch, Queen Elizabeth II.

data.title: Liberty
data.definition: A name of Latin origin, the name Liberty derives from the Latin 'Libertas' and of course, means 'freedom'. Whether you've rescued a pup or just have a dog who loves to break out and run wild, this might make a good choice for them.

data.title: Lilo
data.definition: Famous for the duo in Lilo and Stitch, the name is a girls name of German origin that means 'generous one', however, the name Lilo is used primarily in Hawaiian culture.

data.title: Lily
data.definition: A name of Latin origin, the name Lily is used to reference the ever popular flower with a meaning of  ‘purity’ and ‘innocence’. A great name option to represent the sweet nature of our best furry friends!

data.title: Lindo
data.definition: In Spanish, Lindo means 'cute' which we're pretty sure goes for every pup out there!

data.title: Little John
data.definition: This name is of Scottish and English origin and was used to reference one who is smaller or younger of the same name. This is a great pet name to symbolise a small pup.

data.title: Lobo
data.definition: The Spanish word for 'wolf' might be a great fit for Siberian Huskies, Alaskan Malamutes and other lupine-like breeds with that wolfish face.

data.title: Loki
data.definition:
We may associate this name with Thor’s mischievous step brother from the Marvel franchise, but trust us, this name has great qualities associated with it!

Loki comes from Norse mythology, and is the shape-shifting, gender-bending god of mischief. Even though the mischievousness of the name is apparent, Loki is also associated with sweet and ‘airy’ like qualities.

data.title: Lola
data.definition: The name "Lola" has Spanish origins and is often considered a diminutive form of the name "Dolores," which means "sorrows" or "suffering." However, Lola is also commonly used as an independent name with a playful and lively connotation. It would be a fitting choice for a dog owner seeking a name that reflects their furry friend's energetic and happy personality.

data.title: Lotta
data.definition: This is a lovely name; Lotta means 'free woman'. The name is a shortened version of Charlotte, and would suit any escape artist or pup who just loves to feel the wind in their fur!

data.title: Lottie
data.definition: This is a lovely name; Lotta means 'free woman'. The name is a shortened version of Charlotte, and would suit any escape artist or pup who just loves to feel the wind in their fur!

data.title: Louie
data.definition: Louie means 'famous warrior', and whilst we don't want any dog fights, it might make a good name for those pups with the heart of a warrior. For you movie buffs you will know that King Louie is a character in the Disney film The Jungle Book.

data.title: Lucky
data.definition: Whether you've adopted a pet and made this their lucky day, or just feel lucky to have brought your perfect pup into your life, this is a super cheerful, optimistic name!

data.title: Lulu
data.definition: Lulu is a name with a variety of lovely meanings, such as ‘precious’, peaceful’, and ‘calm’.

data.title: Luna
data.definition: This dreamy name comes from the Italian meaning 'moon', and was also the name of the Roman Goddess of the Moon. You might also know it from the quirky Harry Potter chracter, Luna Lovegood. This dog name topped the 2021 charts for most popular dog names in the UK.

data.title: Lupin
data.definition: Lupin is closely associated to the old canine ancestor, the wolf, and might suit dogs with lupine charactersitics like the Siberian Husky or Alaskan Malamute! Of course, Lupin was also one of Harry Potter's former professors, friends, and a member of the Order of the Phoenix; and he was a werewolf.

data.title: Lyra
data.definition: The name Lyra comes from the legendary lyre from Greek mythology. It was said when Orpheus played on it, it could move animals, trees and the earth itself! The name has also found fame as it is the name of Lyra Silvertongue in Jack Thorne’s His Dark Materials.

data.title: Mabel
data.definition: Mabel comes from Latin origins and means 'lovable' - what a perfect name for your pup!

data.title: Maddie
data.definition: Maddie can mean 'strength in battle', so might make a good match for those pups who NEVER lose a game of tug of war - they don't need to know we're giving in do they?

data.title: Maggie
data.definition: A name of Greek origin, Maggie translates to ‘pearl’. Often associated with its longer version, Margaret (and possibly Britain's first female PM!), in its short form it's a cute name for little treasures.

data.title: Mai Tai
data.definition: A delicious cocktail, and a fun name choice - The Mai Tai got its name from Tahitian language, and it means 'great' or 'excellent', so it could literally apply to ANY dog!

data.title: Maisy
data.definition: Maisie has its roots in Scotland, and means 'pearl' - a cute choice for any little treasure coming into your household.

data.title: Maleficent
data.definition: The name was created by Disney as the used it in their film Sleeping Beauty. Maleficent means 'causing harm'. This name would be a great name for a dog of black or dark brown colour.

data.title: Mandy
data.definition: A name of Latin origin, Mandy translates to ‘loveable’ and couldn’t be more suited to your pup; as whose pup isn’t loveable? OK, chewed socks and sleepless nights aside!

data.title: Marie
data.definition: Marie is a traditional French name which came from the Latin 'stella maris', which means 'star of the sea'. Also the name of white fur, pink bowed Marie from the Aristocats - perfect for a white coat pup.

data.title: Marley
data.definition: A name of British origin, Marley translates to ‘pleasant wood’ and ‘marshy meadow’. It's probably most well known in the dog world as the hero of 'Marley and Me', a true story of the adopted labrador who changes a happily married couple's life with his naughty antics.

data.title: Marlin
data.definition: Marlin is a male name of Welsh origin. Derived from Myrddin, who was a figure in Welsh mythology, this name translates to “land near the lake”, if your pup loves exploring the great outdoors, then this is the perfect name for them!

data.title: Mars
data.definition: Mars is known as the God of War, and is the name of the 'Red Planet', both strong and bold references. Of course it's also the name of a popular chocolate snack; a sweeter option!

data.title: Marshmallow
data.definition: Marshmallow is of course the soft, fluffy sweet - a perfect name for any fluffballs coming into your life too.

data.title: Marta
data.definition: This is a name of Latin origin meaning 'lady' or 'mistress of the house'. This would be a great name for a female pup that is the ruler of the household but also a loving lap dog too!

data.title: Mary
data.definition:
The female name Mary was derived from the ancient Hebrew name Miriam. Miriam is famous in the Old Testament of the Bible and is the sister of Moses.

 

Most famously the name of everyone's favourite supercalifragilisticexpialidocious nanny, Mary Poppins!

data.title: Matcha
data.definition: Matcha is the green powder of ground green tea leaves, and is used to make drinks in Japanese culture. A trendy and punchy little name for firecracker pups in your life.

data.title: Matilda
data.definition: A name of German origin, Matilda translates to ‘battle-mighty’. It's probably most well-known as the protagonist of Roald Dahl's famous book 'Matilda' - a girl who was also unafraid of backing down!

data.title: Maui
data.definition: This is a male name of Hawaiian origin, which means 'god of fire'. This is a great name for red, or brown breeds.

data.title: Maverick
data.definition: Made famous from the Top Gun film, Maverick is a male name that means 'independently minded'. This is a great name for a male dog that is independent, full of fun, but also calm in the eye of the storm!

data.title: Mavis
data.definition: Mavis is a girls name of French origin that means 'songbird', this would be particulary suited to those pups that just don’t know when to stop using those vocal cords.

data.title: Max
data.definition: Max; short, sweet, and simple. This is a shortened name of Maximillian, which means 'the greatest'. A perfect name for a brilliant best friend.

data.title: Maya
data.definition: Maya is such a pretty name with multiple meanings depending on the origin; it can mean 'dream' or 'illusion', whilst from its Greek origins it means ‘good mother’ (Maya was the mother of the Greek God Hermes, son of Zeus), and in Maori it means 'courage'. Whichever association speaks to you, it's a great choice.

data.title: Meara
data.definition: When naming your pup, finding the right name that describes them and suits their personalities, can be challenging. Do you have a dog that is always happy, wagging their tail and always looks like they’re smiling? The name Meara is a great name choice for your pet. Irish roots and meaning ‘jolly’, ‘jovial’, and ‘merry’, there is not better name to use for your excited pet.

data.title: Meeko
data.definition: Meeko is a male name of Irish origin, meaning 'strength', 'wise' and 'great'. The name was made famous by Disney's Pocahontas and her raccoon friend Meeko.

data.title: Megara
data.definition: This is a girls name of Greek origin that means 'great', Megara in Greek mythology is the first wife of Hercules. A great match if you have a male and female pup in your life!

data.title: Mercury
data.definition: Mercury was the name of the Roman God of travellers and tradesmen, and is also the name of our solar system's speediest planet. A shortened version 'Merc' might be a good fit for runners like Greyhounds, Salukis or Whippets.

data.title: Merida
data.definition: Merida is a name of Latin origin that means 'one who has achieved a high honour'. Made famous by Disney character, Princess Merida from the movie Brave - perfect for that independent, ginger coat dog in your life!

data.title: Merlin
data.definition: You might best know Merlin as the most famous wizard of all time, and King Arthur's mentor, but did you know the name actually comes from the Welsh for 'sea fortress' too?

data.title: Merry
data.definition: Merry Christmas! Not quite, this is both a male and female name of English origin, meaning 'lighthearted' and 'happy'. If you have a pup that born around the festive period, this would be a great name for them.

data.title: Mickey
data.definition: Oh boy! There's no Mickey more famous than Mickey Mouse, the iconic character at the centre of Walt Disney's legacy. One to use if you have two dogs to be the Minnie to their Mickey. Did you know the name Mickey means 'like God', and symbolises kindness, humour and sensitivity?

data.title: Mika
data.definition: Mika has roots in lots of languages, and sometimes translates to ‘beautiful fragrance’. We think it's a fun, spunky name for a pup, and what's better in the world than that puppy smell? We'll choose to forge the times they have rolled in something ghastly!

data.title: Mila
data.definition: From its Spanish origins, Mila translates to ‘miracles’. It can also mean ‘gracious’ and ‘dear one’. Our pups can be little miracles in our lives, so we think this name is perfect for your special furball.

data.title: Miley
data.definition: They do come in like wrecking balls when they're pups don't they - Miley makes a great name for strong, unapologetic pups with an independent spirit!

data.title: Milo
data.definition: Milo is one of our most popular male names, and is said to be derived from many origins. In Slavic languages, 'mil' means 'dear' or 'beloved'. However, there are those that believe the name is derived from the Latin word 'miles' which means 'soldier'. Whichever definition you choose for Milo, this is a sweet, and warm name for your pup.

data.title: Mimosa
data.definition: Mimosa is of course, the delicious sparkling cocktail, but also means 'tropical tree'. Most people that use the name Mimosa shorten it to 'Mimi', which can also mean 'rebellious'. If you have a pup that is sweet, full of energy, and liable to do their own thing, this is a great name choice for them!

data.title: Minnie
data.definition: Minnie is a popular name used to describe something small. With the name meaning 'of the mind' and 'intellect', it's perfect for the small but mighty pet in your life. Famously the namesake of Minnie Mouse, the better and more fashionable half of Mickey Mouse!

data.title: Missy
data.definition: Missy is a short form of 'Melissa', which means 'bee'. The name is famous for being the name of the mythological nymph and princess, Melissa, who transformed into a bee after collecting honey.

data.title: Mixie
data.definition: Mixie is a popular female dog name. The name would be great if you have a dog that is a mixture of breeds, or colours.

data.title: Moana
data.definition: Another great Disney heroine, Moana is a girls name that doesn’t really need an introduction! The name Moana is a Hawaiian name meaning 'ocean'.

data.title: Mocha
data.definition: Mocha will probably make you think of the sweetened chocolate coffee; so it's a great choice for brown pooches. It's also a combination of two words, Mehr and Nisa, which mean 'beautiful' and 'faithful'.

data.title: Mochi
data.definition: Mochis are delicious Japanese cake balls made of a kind of soft and chewy rice, often wrapped around yummy fillings like ice cream. They come in all kinds of flavours and colours, making this a super fun name for little fluffballs!

data.title: Molly
data.definition: Molly is a name of Irish origin,  which means 'star of the sea'. There are many well known Mollys in Gaelic folklore, and it is perfect for a furry friend who loves the waves.

data.title: Morpheus
data.definition: Morpheus is the god most associated with sleep and dreams, and might be well-suited to breeds who like a nap like the Greyhound, Bulldog or Saint Bernard!

data.title: Moxie
data.definition: When your dog's got a 'lot of moxie', you sure know about it! Meaning 'energy' or 'nerve', this is a great name for a driven dog who knows their own mind!

data.title: Muffin
data.definition: There's no great mystery behind the meaning of Muffin; they're sweet, moreish and well-loved; just like our pups! If you've got a little muffin in your life, why not say it as you see it?

data.title: Mulan
data.definition: Another strong and brave Disney princess name, Mulan means 'magnolia', which is why magnolia flowers are so prominent in that movie.

data.title: Murdoch
data.definition: A popular Scottish name and surname, the meaning of Murdoch is 'sea', making this an unusual choice for water-loving breeds like the Irish Water Spaniel, Newfoundland and Otterhound.

data.title: Mushu
data.definition: Mushu is a male name from Muslim origin, meaning 'very pleasing'. This is a great name for a dog who's more like your best friend, as they bring a lot of joy and happiness to our lives.

data.title: Mya
data.definition: Mya is of Greek origin and means 'great' or 'spring' - a name full of optimism and cheer!

data.title: Myrtle
data.definition: The myrtle plant expodes with little white firework flowers and aromatic berries, and being ruled by Venus, is associated with peace, love and youth. It might sound a little old-fashioned, but we think it's time to bring it back!

data.title: Nacho
data.definition: Who doesn't love a nacho - the cheesier the better! If you're one for a piquante dip to go with your snack, this name might suit as Nacho is actually a short-form of the Spanish Ignacio, meaning 'firey'!

data.title: Nala
data.definition: Nala has many meanings and is commonly used across many African cultures, from 'Queen' and 'successful' to 'drink of water' and 'honeybee'. You may remember it most famously as the name of the Lioness in The Lion King.

data.title: Nana
data.definition: You may know the furry Nana we're thinking of; the Newfoundland Nana from Peter Pan who was actually based on a dog of the same breed owned by author J.M.Barrie! Perfect for those natural-born caretakers.

data.title: Nani
data.definition: This is a female name of Greek origin, meaning 'grace'. In other cultures such as Japanese and Hawaiian the name means 'beautiful'. This is a lovely soft girls name, intended to bring out the best in your pup.

data.title: Napoleon
data.definition: A name steeped in French history, Napoleon made his name during the French Revolution - a great moniker for diminuative pups with big personalities!

data.title: Nara
data.definition: Do you have a dog that is soft, caring and kind? Well, this name would suit them perfectly. With roots in Japanese, Hindi, and Celtic culture, the nae Nara, means ‘happiness’. Traditionally used as a surname in Japanese culture and meaning ‘male God’ in Hindi, this is a great name to symbolise strength, kindness and happiness in your pet.

data.title: Naveen
data.definition: The name Naveen is primarily a male name of Indian origin that means 'New'. This is a great name to sybolise the new relationship you and your pup will have together.

data.title: Nebula
data.definition: This is a name of Latin origin, which means 'mist', also aptly referring to the hazy mass of cosmic dust around stars. This is a really unique name choice for you grey or white breed owners out there.

data.title: Ned
data.definition: A male name of Old English origin meaning 'guardian of the riches' or 'wealthy'. Ned has usually been used as a pet name for Edward, and Edmund, but is becoming a sweet and short male name in its own right. For those Game of Thrones lovers out there, the late and respectable Ned Stark wore this name with pride.

data.title: Nefertiti
data.definition: This Egyptian name is a bit of a mouthful, and is most famously known as the name of the Great Queen and wife of the Eyptian Pharaoh Akhenaten - but it does mean 'the perfect one has arrived', so is possibly worth saying!

data.title: Nell
data.definition: The meaning of Nell is "shining light"; a bright name for the sunshine in your life.

data.title: Nellie
data.definition: Nellie is a short-form version of Cornelia, and means 'sun ray'. We all need a little sun in our lives, and what better than to name your dog after that?

data.title: Nelson
data.definition: Nelson is a strong, solid name that means 'champion', and has been attached to the likes of activist and freedom fighter Nelson Mandela. Quite the legacy to live up to; and we're convinced your pup is up to the task!

data.title: Nemo
data.definition: Nemo is boy's name of Greek origin meaning "nobody", but you may be most familiar with it as the name of lovable clownfish Nemo in Finding Nemo. This name is a great one for an adopted pet whose been given a new loving home.

data.title: Neptune
data.definition: Neptune was of course, the Roman God of the sea - so this name is perfect for pups who just can't stay out of the water, or were even bred to be completely at home in every pond, river and wave they find!

data.title: Nero
data.definition: The name Nero is of Latin origin and means 'vigorous' and 'strong'. Made most famous by the Emperor of the same name, Nero was infamously known for his debauchery and stern demeanour. One for the serious pups who mean business!

data.title: Nessie
data.definition: Most well known as the affectionate name for Scotland's fabled Loch Ness Monster, Nessie actually also means 'pure' or 'chaste'. It also has Greek origins, being short for Agnes and meaning 'lamb'.

data.title: Newton
data.definition: Newton's actual meaning isn't very original, given it simply comes from the English meaning ‘new town’. It is perhaps most famously known to be the name of the mathematician, astronomer, theologian, physicist and author, Sir Isaac Newton, who formulated the laws of gravity and motion which, any dog owner who's trying to perfect the commands 'down' and 'stay' will be very familiar with!

data.title: Nico
data.definition: Nico is a short, sharp Italian name which means 'victory of the people'.

data.title: Nigel
data.definition: Nigel is a boys name of mixed origin. The name means 'champion' and is a great name to inspire and motivate your pup.

data.title: Nina
data.definition: This is another name for those who own a small breed of pup. It is of Spanish origin and means 'little girl'.

data.title: Noel
data.definition: This is one to get you in the festive spirit. Noel, is derived from the old French word 'nael', which means 'born of Christmas'. So, if you love Christmas and want to enjoy it all year round, then why not name your pup Noel?

data.title: Noodle
data.definition: We love this name for dogs who like to wiggle, long slinky pups, and those with long curly locks - it's full of fun!

data.title: Nori
data.definition: Nori is a type of Japanese seaweed, and can be more romantically translated to 'ocean moss'! One for the sea-loving pups, or just owners who are obsessed with sushi!

data.title: Nova
data.definition: Nova stems from the Latin word 'novus' which means 'new'. This is a great name to symbolise a new beginning!

data.title: Nugget
data.definition: You probably immediately think of the chicken dipping pieces so often found in fast food restaurants when you hear Nugget, but the actual origin of the word came from lumps of gold! A tongue-in-cheek name for the most valuable little treasure in your life.

data.title: Nymeria
data.definition: If you're a Game of Thrones fan, this one is for you. Nymeria was of course one of the six Direwolf pups adopted by the Stark children, herself belonging to Arya. The name literally means ‘a warrior queen’. If you have a pup with a brave spirit and huge sense of adventure, this is the perfect name for them!

data.title: Nyx
data.definition: Nyx means 'night' in Greece, and was known as the goddess of the night and the daughter of chaos! A seriously unusual name for those looking for something darkly mystical!

data.title: Oakley
data.definition: This male name is of British origin and simply means 'meadow of oak trees'. A great way to celebrate your pup's love of nature and the outdoors.

data.title: Obi
data.definition: Obi is actually a Nigerian name meaning 'heart', though you might better know it as belonging to the legendary Jedi Master Obi-Wan Kenobi of the Star Wars stories. One for dogs with great heart, bravery and loyalty.

data.title: Odie
data.definition: Odie is best known as the name of Garfield's canine companion - part best friend, part victim to Garfield's various plans, this is a great name if you've got a cat at home and are looking to make them a new best friend too!

data.title: Odin
data.definition: There's a lot of energy behind this name, taken from the Norse God of wisdom, poetry and war - also known as the 'Lord of Frenzy'! Whilst there's a dash of strength and power here, he was also known for his mental agility, so a good one for the extra smart pups!

data.title: Olaf
data.definition: Do you want to build a snowman? Olaf is a Norse name meaning 'relic'. Famous for being the name of the hilarious and lovable snowman from the Disney film Frozen,as well as being associated with the patron saint of Norway St. Olaf.

data.title: Olive
data.definition: Olive unsurprisingly, comes from the olive tree which is known as a symbol of fruitfulness and, when a branch is handed out, one of peace!

data.title: Oliver
data.definition: Oliver unsurprisingly, comes from the olive tree which is known as a symbol of fruitfulness and, when a branch is handed out, one of peace!

data.title: Olivia
data.definition: Olivia unsurprisingly, comes from the olive tree which is known as a symbol of fruitfulness and, when a branch is handed out, one of peace!

data.title: Oreo
data.definition: This one is perfect for black and white pooches like Dalmatians - the iconic snack is known for its dark biscuit and white cream, so makes a tongue-in-cheek name for dogs with the same colouring!

data.title: Oscar
data.definition: Oscar is boy's name of English, Irish origin meaning 'God spear' or 'deer-lover or champion warrior'. In addition to being a top name in Sweden, where the name has connections to royalty, the name has many famous attachments too, such as poet and playwright Oscar Wilde.

data.title: Otis
data.definition: Otis means 'wealthy', which no new dog owner feels when those puppy eyes keep the new toys coming in!

data.title: Oz
data.definition: Off to see the wizard? The wonderful wizard of Oz? This great and powerful wizard, also known as Oz, the Great and Terrible, and Great and Powerful Oz, is the great wizard from L. Frank Baums fictional musical, The Wizard of Oz. The name Oz is of Hebrew origin and is a boys name, meaning strength, power and courage.

data.title: Ozzy
data.definition: Ozzy immediately makes us think of the head of the rebellious Osbourne clan; part of 70s heavy meal band Black Sabbath, and known as The Prince of Darkness! Perhaps a tongue-in-cheek name for any dark-coloured dogs, or those with a taste for danger! The name's origin actually comes from Old Norse and means 'God's power' or 'bear god', and is associated with being the shortened version of Oswald or Osmand  - so it could be a good name for those big teddy bears too.

data.title: Pablo
data.definition: Pablo is a name of Mexican origin, and it means 'little'. Pablo has ties with the name Paul, which means 'small' and 'humble'. The name has had many famous forbears including Pablo Picasso and Pablo Escobar, so might  be a great choice for any pup on the more adventurous side of life.

data.title: Pacha
data.definition: Evolved from the Latin word 'pax', which means 'peace'. Pacha is a well known surname. Characteristics associated with the name Pacha are kind, loving and brave. You might know the name Pacha from the leader of the village in Disney's Emperor's New Groove!

data.title: Paisley
data.definition: The name Paisley is of Scottish origin, and means 'church' although it's perhaps more commonly known  as the famous teardrop-design Indian fabric.

data.title: Pan
data.definition: This name is perfect for mischievious pups, as Pan was a Greek god known for his impish streak! He was also the God of shepherds and pastures, so it also makes a great short, sharp name for working Collies, Sheepdogs and Cattle dogs.

data.title: Pandora
data.definition: Getting a new puppy can sometimes feel like you've opened Pandora's Box - the sleepless nights, the potty training, the razor-sharp teeth - however, it's also a new beginning full of joy, hope and adventure. Just like the famous namesake, Pandora means 'all gifts' - the good, the bad and the ultimately wonderful!

data.title: Parker
data.definition: Parker is a name of Old English origin meaning 'keeper of the park' - a perfect name for pooches who rule the lands of the local park!

data.title: Pascal
data.definition: Pascal is name of French and English origin, which means 'of the passover'. Pascal was historically used for sons born at Easter, so if you have a chocolate coloured dog, then is a great name option.

data.title: Patch
data.definition: A classic name for any dog with a distinctive marking on their bodies, Patch is a cute choice for them whether it's a pirate's eye or a patch of colour elsewhere!

data.title: Paul
data.definition: Paul is a Latin male name that means 'humble' or 'small'. Most famous for the singer Paul McCartney, who was a member of the Beatles. This name is a great option for any pup, big or small.

data.title: Peach
data.definition: If your dog  is 'a real peach', this could make a really sweet girl's name and is of French, German and English origins.

data.title: Peanut
data.definition: Of course this would be a cute little name for smaller breeds; although Peanut could also be a funny choice for giants with a sense of humour!

data.title: Pearl
data.definition: Pearl's are hewn out of grit and sand and symbolise purity, wisdom and transformation, so this might make a lovely choice for a rescue pup or adoptee ready to turn over a new leaf!

data.title: Pebbles
data.definition: Pebbles of course refer to small rocks, so makes a cute name for a little dog, but is perhaps best known as the name of the toddler in the Flinstones family.

data.title: Pedro
data.definition: Our pets can be the rocks in our lives; they give us purpose and meaning, so why not name them after something that means just that? The name Pedro is of Spanish origin, and means 'rock'. It is the Spanish form of the popular name Peter.

data.title: Pegasus
data.definition: Pegasus is boy's name meaning 'from spring water'. In Greek mythology Pegasus is a white winged stallion, who sprang from the blood of Medusa, followimg her beheaded by Perseus.

data.title: Pepe
data.definition: Got a pup with some pep in their step? This Spanish name might be the right fit for them!

data.title: Pepper
data.definition: Do you have pup that is a mixture of black, white and grey? We have the perfect name for them; Pepper, a name of American origin, means 'hot spice'.

data.title: Pepsi
data.definition: Pepsi, in Latin means 'cooking' and 'digestion'. But we all know that the name Pepsi is famous for the drink. This is a great name for those brown pups in our lives, and the ones that have that little 'bounce' in their step.

data.title: Percy
data.definition: Percy is a name of French origin, that means "one who pierces the valley". The name is derived from Percival, both strong names for your pup! Percy features in Pocahontas as Governor Ratcliffe's pup.

data.title: Perdita
data.definition: Best known as the Mama dog in Disney's 101 Dalmatians, Perdita is extremely elegant, polite and beautiful, though can sometimes come across as a little serious! She's a fierce protector of her pack.

data.title: Pickle
data.definition: A cute name, usually used to describe someone that is sweet in nature, and small in size, making it a great name for those smaller furry friends with a giant heart.

data.title: Piglet
data.definition: For those Winnie the Pooh fans out there, this is a great name to give your kind and maybe slightly shy pup. Piglet was the name for Pooh's best friend; brave, kind and caring, so what better name to give a pup that has all these characteristics?

data.title: Pip
data.definition: The name Pip is of English origin, and most popular for being the chosen name of the main character from Great Expectations. The name pip means 'lover of horses'.

data.title: Piper
data.definition: Piper is an English name that means someone who plays the musical pipe, flute, or bagpipes. If you've got a pup that just won't pipe down, this might be the right name for them!

data.title: Pixel
data.definition: A pixel is a tiny little part of a much bigger digital image - the smallest element making up a whole - we think this is a super cute name for a little dog who might just be the missing piece in your family portrait.

data.title: Pixie
data.definition: Pixie is a name used for a mythological creature. It means 'fairy''; think of Tinkerbell, or the naughty pixies from Harry Potter - making it the perfect name for a smaller pup with a lot of cheek!

data.title: Pluto
data.definition: Pluto is the name of the Greek God of the underworld, but if that feels a bit dark for your pet, it was also the name of Mickey Mouse's pet dog and ultimate sidekick! First appearing in the 1930s, he's thought to be a Bloodhound and although he gets himself into some scrapes, he's also smart enough to get out of trouble.

data.title: Pocahontas
data.definition: Just around the river bend! Pocahontas meaning 'playful one', is the beautiful name given to the favourite daughter of Powhatan, the ruler of the Algonquian-speaking tribes, in the Disney film Pocahontas.

data.title: Pongo
data.definition: Fans of 101 Dalmatians will no doubt know Pongo as the Papa Dog of the pack; a relaxed, playful but extremely loyal dog who's love for his master Roger is unbreakable.

data.title: Poppy
data.definition: A cute flower name for dogs, Poppy is a great choice for dogs large and small. These bright red flowers with dark centers are strikingly beautiful. The name itself is short and sweet, and easy for others to learn—including your dog!

data.title: Porter
data.definition: Porter, a French male name meaning, 'doorkeeper'. This name is a great one for those pups that are great draft excluders, or just like to be waiting at the door for any and all visitors.

data.title: Precious
data.definition: A girl's name of Latin origin meaning 'of great value or highly esteemed'. Our pets are precious to us and so they should be named!

data.title: Prince
data.definition: This English male name of course, means 'royal son'. It's self-explanatory; if your new arrival is in for the royal treatment, this might be the name for them!

data.title: Princess
data.definition: This English female name of course, means 'royal daughter'. It's self-explanatory; if your new arrival is in for the royal treatment, this might be the name for them!

data.title: Puck
data.definition:
The name Puck has American origins, and is usually used as a name to symbolise a pet that is mischevious or has the character of a bouncy and hyper pet. They might also be as small as a hockey puck!

Puck was the name given to the character from A Midsummer Nights Dream.

data.title: Pumbaa
data.definition: The name Pumbaa comes from the Swahili language, and translates to 'slow-witted' or 'stupid'. Poor Pumbaa seems to draw the short straw when it comes to name meanings. Despite Pumbaas representation in The Lion King, as being slow, he is a great and fierce friend.

data.title: Pumpkin
data.definition: For you Halloween fans out there, or the autumnal vibe chasers, this is a great name choice. The name Pumpkin of course is the name given to the root vegetable, however has become increasingly popular as a pet name over the years.

data.title: Quake
data.definition: This is a great name for large dog breeds, this male name means 'tremble' or 'earthquake'. Think of your big pup running towards you - this name makes sense now!

data.title: Queen
data.definition: Another one for the royalty category, the female name Queen is of English origin, and is the title for a female royal who sits on the throne. Do you have a female pup that acts very much like the Queen of the household? This name is a match made in heaven.

data.title: Quest
data.definition: The name simply means a search or pursuit of something; but it certainly has a touch of the epicabout it! The name embodies a sense of adventure, curiosity, and a determined spirit.

data.title: Quincy
data.definition: Funnily enough, this name is commonly used as a name in the Equine sphere, but has become increasingly popular as a dog name. The name is Latin male name meaning 'the fifth'. In Old Roman, the five symbolized human life and a touch of the rebellious.

data.title: Rain
data.definition: If there's one thing we can guarantee, it's that you're about to become much more familiar with being out in the rain than ever before! Pups need to go out in all weather; and though this name might sound gloomy on first read, we think it's refreshing!

data.title: Raja
data.definition: This is a great name on our gender-neutral list. From Arabic origin, Raja. The name is said to have a few meanings, and among those are ‘optimism’ and ‘happiness’. We all known that our four-legged friends bring happiness and joy to our lives, so what better name to give your pup then one that means just that?

data.title: Ralph
data.definition: The name Ralph is a golden oldie, and comes from the German meaning 'wolf counsel'.

data.title: Rambo
data.definition: Rambo is a popular and fitting name that is given to many military and protection dogs, the name referring to their protective characteristics and of course, Sylvester Stallone's iconic character in the films of the same name!

data.title: Ramen
data.definition: Ramen; yes like the delicious noodle dish of Japanese origin, where it translates literally to 'a noodle'. Great for long skinny pups, wiggly pups, and those with lots of long or curly hair!

data.title: Randy
data.definition: The name Randy is derived from the English word "rand," which means "to run swiftly" or "to be full of energy." A great name for dogs with high energy levels and a playful nature, who love to constantly  be on the move, are always ready for a game or a long run in the park.

data.title: Ranger
data.definition: A French name meaning 'forest guardian', Ranger refers to a person who patrols and protects a specific area, such as a park or a forest. A great name for dogs known for their bravery, vigilance, and loyalty, with a natural instinct to guard and protect their family and territory, making them an excellent watchdog.

data.title: Rapunzel
data.definition: Rapunzel, Rapunzel, let down your hair! Do you have a female pup whose hair never seems to stop growing and has a real sense of adventure? Well we think this is the perfect name for them! The name means 'rampion'.

data.title: Rascal
data.definition: This is a great name for a mischevious and playful pup. The name Rascal is often used to describe someone who is lively and sometimes naughty! This name would suit a dog with a spirited and adventurous nature, always ready for fun and games, and finding creative ways to entertain themselves!

data.title: Ratcliffe
data.definition: Ratcliffe is a name of British origin, meaning 'red cliff'. This would be a great name for a large dog breed, or for fans of Disney's Pocahontas.

data.title: Ratigan
data.definition: Ratigan is most famous for being the name of the evil rat in the Disney film The Great Mouse Detective.Ratigan would be a great name for a black dog.

data.title: Raven
data.definition: Ravens are intelligent and enigmatic birds known for their dark feathers and distinctive calls; not to mention their intelligence!  A great name for dog's with a keen intellect and a sharp sense of awareness, and those who are quick learners, easily picking up new commands and tricks!

data.title: Rayne
data.definition: This is a female name of Scandiavian origin and is connected to rain - carrying a sense of grace, beauty, and serenity.

data.title: Rebel
data.definition: The name Rebel carries a strong and defiant connotation, often associated with individuals who challenge the status quo and march to the beat of their own drum! If you've got a dog with a spirited and independent nature, and a bold and fearless attitude this could be the right name for you!

data.title: Reggie
data.definition: The name Reggie is of Latin origin and means 'rulers advisor', the name is often used as a short form of the name Reginald.

data.title: Reina
data.definition: Of Spanish origin, this name means 'Queen' and symbolises great power and strength!

data.title: Remy
data.definition:
Remy, a French name that means 'oarsman' is the name of the fifth century saint.  Famously the name of tiny little rat chef Remy in Pixar's Ratatouille.

This is a great name for a strong and brave pup that wont let anything get in their way - and perhaps a love for food!

data.title: Rex
data.definition: The name Rex carries a regal and powerful connotation, as it is derived from the Latin word for "king" or "ruler."  A classic name for dogs with a commanding presence and a natural air of authority.

data.title: Ricky
data.definition: The name Ricky is a diminutive form of the name Richard, which has Germanic origins and means "brave ruler" or "powerful leader." Ricky might suggest a dog with a slightly more cheerful and lively personality, whilst still keeping a watchful eye over the home!

data.title: Ringo
data.definition: This is a Japanese name meaning 'apple', and has most famously been given to Richard Starkey aka, Ringo Star, the musician and drummer from The Beatles. The name was given to him due to the excess amount of rings he wore.

data.title: Rio
data.definition: Rio is a male name of Spanish origin. The name means 'river'. So, get your waterproof pants on and get out there with your nature-inspired pup!

data.title: Rita
data.definition: Rita has origins in Spain where it's a shortened form of Margarita, meaning 'pearl'. If you want your pup to know that the world is their oyster to explore, enjoy and love, this is a great name for them.

data.title: Ritz
data.definition: The name Ritz carries a sense of elegance, sophistication, and luxury. It is often associated with high-class hotels and glamorous lifestyles. If you've got a dog with a refined and graceful demeanor, confident presence and even an air of sophistication, Ritz would be a stand-out name.

data.title: Rocco
data.definition: Rocco is an Italian name meaning 'rest' and 'repose'. If you've got a lazy pup on your hands, or one who just loves a snuggle on the sofa, this might be the name for them.

data.title: Rocket
data.definition: Not one for the quiet pups out there, Rocket is a name packed with get-up-and-go! If your dog is full of energy, agility, and has a particularly a dynamic nature or explosive ability to take on life, this is a great choice.

data.title: Rocky
data.definition: Strong, sturdy and dependable; that's what we think of when we hear Rocky - and of course it brings to mind Sylvester Stallone's Oscar-nominated role as Rocky Baboa in the 1976 film of the same name! If you've got a confident pup on your hands, this might be just the name to match.

data.title: Rolly
data.definition: Rolly is a male name of German origin, which means 'famed land'. The name Rolly is derived from the name Roland. And is often used to describe the fat rolls on dogs. So if you're dog is of a breed that has rolls in their skin, this is a great name for them!

data.title: Rolo
data.definition: In Old Norse and German, Rolo can mean 'famed wolf', which is quite an important moniker for any dog! Of course, it also makes us think of the famous 'last' chocolate - maybe one of those who always get the last scrap off your plate!

data.title: Romeo
data.definition: Let's hope you don't spend your time together shouting 'wherefore art thou, Romeo?' - runaways aside, this is a lovely romantic name which is of course most famously known from the Shakespearean play.

data.title: Rona
data.definition: A great name for strong and stoic girls, this Norse name originally means 'mighty strength' and is a popular Scottish choice as it shares its name with one of the country's remote islands.

data.title: Roo
data.definition: Roo is English Girl name, famous for the young kangaroo in the childrens story Winnie the Pooh. The name Roo means 'companion', 'friend' and 'neighbour'.

data.title: Rose
data.definition: The name "Rose" is derived from the flower of the same name, which is often associated with beauty, elegance, and grace. Perhaps one for canine companions who are gentle, delicate, of course, beautiful!

data.title: Rosie
data.definition: Rosie is a feminine name of Latin origin that means "rose". There are connections with the Latin name Rosa, as well as often being used as a nickname for Rosemary and Roseline. This sweet floral name is a classic choice for your pet, to symbolise the blossoming of a great life together.

data.title: Rover
data.definition: Not only is Rover one of the most classic names around, it's also the name of the curious little robot geologists roaming around Mars. The name is often associated with exploration, adventure, a free-spirit and adventurous nature. If you've got a curious pup, or are just looking for a traditional name, this is a great choice.

data.title: Roxy
data.definition: A name of Persian in origin, Roxy is a girls name that means 'dawn', 'bright' and 'star'. The name Roxy symbolises the beginning of exciting adventures. Which is a great name for your new pup.

data.title: Ruby
data.definition: The name "Ruby" is derived from the gemstone of the same name. It is a precious stone known for its deep red color and is associated with passion, energy, and vitality. The most famous ruby is the Sunrise Ruby, mined in Burma and worth over $30 million dollars! One for those one-in-a-million pups worth their weight in gold!

data.title: Rudy
data.definition: Rudy, a name most commonly associated with the name Rudolph, is a German male name which means 'famous wolf'. If you have a pup that howls like a wolf and could well be the size of one, then this is a fab name.

data.title: Rufus
data.definition: The name Rufus is a boy's name which means 'red-head', and would be a great name for those ginger pups in your life.

data.title: Rune
data.definition: This is a Norse name derived from the word rŭn, meaning ‘secret’. We've long known puppy-parenting is the secret to happiness; now if someone could just tell us how to stop them chewing...!

data.title: Rusty
data.definition: Rusty, similar to Rufus, means red-headed and is a great choice for those red, copper or ginger-coloured pups.

data.title: Sabrina
data.definition: This enchanting name has us twitching with teenage nostalgia for the well-loved witch, though it's also meant to mean 'legendary princess' which we think works just as well!

data.title: Sadie
data.definition: Sadie has a lovely meaning connected to it's Hebrew origins; 'princess'. If your pup is about to inherit the household (and let's face it, which ones don't end up ruling the roost in one way or another!) this is a lovely choice.

data.title: Saga
data.definition: Saga, a name from Norse mythology which also means ‘she who sees everything’. It is a girl’s name, and is a lovely unusual choice. Saga was the Norse goddess of poetry, storytelling, and history.

data.title: Sage
data.definition: Sage is a lovely name; its origin is Latin, and means ‘wise’. The Greeks and Romans believed that the smoke which came from burnt sage leaves brought wisdom. In some Middle-Eastern cultures it is said that sage leaves brought immortality.

data.title: Sandy
data.definition: Sandy makes us think of beaches, buckets and spades, and desperately trying to towel down mucky pups! It actually means 'defender of mankind', so ideal for your seaside sidekick!

data.title: Sarge
data.definition: Sarge is a boy's name of Old French origin, meaning 'officer', and is a pet name used for Sergeant.

data.title: Sassy
data.definition: Of course this name is perfect for a bold and cheeky pup - ready for a lifetime of adventures!

data.title: Scar
data.definition: This is a male name of English origin, known famously as the evil brother of Mufasa in 'The Lion King', the name unsurprisingly, means 'mark left on the skin'.

data.title: Scarlet
data.definition: Scarlet is a girl's name of French origin and means 'red'; an obvious choice for any russet-hued pups coming into your life.

data.title: Schnitzel
data.definition: No trip to Germany is complete without trying the country's iconic meat dish, the schnitzel! A cute name for little German breeds like the dachshund, schnauzer or munsterlander.

data.title: Scooby
data.definition: Scooby doo, where are you! Yes, we are all familiar with the famous cartoon Great Dane that has excellent tracking skills. Scooby Doo, as a name doesn’t have a set definition, however, the word scooby, derived from the British language, is often used as another word for ‘clue’, which would seem fitting! So, if you have a curious four-legged pet, or just a quizzical one, Scooby would be the perfect name for them.

data.title: Scooter
data.definition: Scooter just means, the 'one who moves fast' - a great name for a pup that runs at the speed of light or has a tendency to have a crazy 5 minutes! Which let's face it, is most of them!

data.title: Scotch
data.definition: This name literally means 'of or from Scotland', and of course, refers to the famous whisky! A great choice for proud Scottish breeds like the Scottish Terrier, West Highland White Terrier, and Skye Terrier.

data.title: Scout
data.definition: Scout is a great name for those pups that love a good adventure, derived from the Old French origin 'to listen'.

data.title: Sebastian
data.definition: The name Sebastian is primarily a male name of Greek origin. Sebastian is derived from the Latin Sebastianus, which was used for a man from the area of Sebaste. Sabastian is famous for the name of the Crab in the animated Disney film, The Little Mermaid.

data.title: Selene
data.definition: Do you love your four-legged friend to the moon and back? Well, if so, this is the name for you. Selene is a Greek name that means ‘the moon goddess’. Characteristics associated with this name are calm, mysterious and quiet.

data.title: Shadow
data.definition: A great name for any grey or black dog breeds, or pups with an air of mystery about them!

data.title: Shelby
data.definition: Meaning 'willow' from its Norse origins, Shelby might make a great name for pups with lengthy, lithe limbs or a lustrous long coat. Its also famously known as the surname for the Shelby family from Peaky Blinders so if your pup's got a little extra personality, this might be for them.

data.title: Shiloh
data.definition: The name "Shiloh" has Hebrew origins and carries various meanings, including "peaceful," "tranquil," and "place of rest." It is often associated with a sense of serenity and harmony, and suggests a canine companion who embodies a calm and gentle nature.

data.title: Shu
data.definition: ‘Good’, ‘pure’, and ‘virtuous; does you pup spring to mind after reading these words? Well, carry on reading to find out the name associated with all these great words. The name Shu, originating from China, is primarily used as a female name.

data.title: Sierra
data.definition: Sierra is a unique name that is associated with wildness and rugged charm. Stemming from the Spanish term for mountain range, it is formed from the Latin words, serra, which means 'saw'.

data.title: Sigrid
data.definition: In Norse this name means a 'beautiful victory', which, when it comes to dog-ownership, might just be gaining back control of the sofa!

data.title: Simon
data.definition: Simon actually means 'to hear' or 'listen' which, as any dog owner dealing with the trials of training will tell you, is not always an easy skill! It's a classic name, and once they've perfected their recall, a fitting one for very good boys.

data.title: Skip
data.definition: Skip takes its name from 'skipper' or 'sea captain', meaning it might be a nice choice for water dogs like the Irish Water Spaniel, Lagotto Romagnolo or Otterhound.

data.title: Sky
data.definition: The name "Sky" of course refers to the celestial expanse above us. It represents vastness, freedom, and a sense of limitless possibilities, and is perfect for an adventurous, free-spirited, and energetic pup.

data.title: Skye
data.definition: Named after the largest of Scotland's Inner Hebrides Islands, Skye is as footloose and free-wheeling a name as the expansive horizon it alludes to!

data.title: Slinky
data.definition: This classic spring-like toy really came into its own when it became the canine star of Toy Story, Slinky the dog. Very Dachshund-like thanks to his extendable body and short legs, he's a playful, opinionated pooch that loves his owner Woody. A tongue-in-cheek name for sausage-owners!

data.title: Smoky
data.definition: The name "Smoky" is often associated with the visual effect of smoke or the color grayish-black, and carries with it a mysterious and alluring quality!

data.title: Snoop
data.definition: This is not really a name as such, but more of a noun which refers to someone that is a bit of a 'busy body'. It might also make you think of American rapper Snoog Dogg; how appropriate! Snoop is a great name for a pup that like to be involved in anything and everything!

data.title: Snoopy
data.definition: Snoopy might be one of the world's most famous pooches and is one of the main members in Peanuts. Beagles would be a natural choice of course, but Snoopy is a characterful name for any breed thanks to his great nature!

data.title: Snow
data.definition: Another name for those white pups out there, Snow suggests a canine companion who is pure, gentle, and brings a sense of beauty and tranquility to those around them.

data.title: Snow White
data.definition: Can you name all 7 dwarves? Snow White is one of the most well known Princesses in Disney history, and what better name to give you beloved white pup than that of a lovely, charming princess?

data.title: Sophia
data.definition: The name Sophia is a female name of Greek origin that means Wisdom. Famous owners of the name have been Sophia Bush and Sophia Loren; perhaps a name for pups with a penchant for the dramatic!

data.title: Sparky
data.definition: Meaning to 'give off sparks', this is a great name to use for your furry bundle of energy that just seems to always be at 100% charge!

data.title: Spike
data.definition: Spike is most famously known as the tough-guy Bulldog in Looney Tunes, usually wearing a scowl alongside his bowler hat!

data.title: Spot
data.definition: Spot the Dog is a classic in childrens storytelling with his distinctive brown spots and tail tip! If you love the vintage vibe of this old school cartoon it might be the name for you, or else perfect for dotty companions like Dalmatians, Border Collies and Corgis.

data.title: Star
data.definition: Choose this modern celestial name, originally from the Latin 'stella' and the Greek 'aster'. If your pup shines brightly in your life, this is a great name for them.

data.title: Stitch
data.definition: Famously known as everyone's favourite mischievous experiment 626 in Disney's Lilo and Stitch. Meaning 'lost and put back together' in Hawaiian, it's the perfect name for a dog who is now part of your ohana.

data.title: Suki
data.definition: Suki is of Japanese origins and means 'loved one'.

data.title: Sultan
data.definition: The name "Sultan" has Arabic origins and is often associated with royalty, power, and authority. It signifies a position of leadership and prominence and suggests a canine companion who is regal, confident, and possesses a commanding presence.

data.title: Summer
data.definition: The name "Summer" is derived from the season of the same name, representing warmth, sunshine, and a vibrant energy - a great name for a pup who is cheerful, lively, and embraces the spirit of the season.

data.title: Sumo
data.definition: The name Sumo is of Japanese origin and means 'wrestler'. If you have a rough and tumble pup then this is a great choice of name for them.

data.title: Sunny
data.definition: Sunny, a female name meaning 'sunshine', 'happy', and 'cheerful', is another great name for a pet that is bubbly, cheerful and full of love.

data.title: Sven
data.definition: The name itself is Old Norse for 'Young Man' or 'young warrior'. Sven is a popular first name in many Scandianvian countries, and also popular as the cuddly reindeer Sven from Disney's Frozen! If you think dogs are better than people, this might be for you...

data.title: Taco
data.definition: The word "Taco" is derived from Mexican cuisine and refers to a traditional dish consisting of a tortilla filled with various ingredients, so we reckon it's could be a fun name for a spicy little addition to your life! If you've got a dog with a zest for life and a sassy side, this might be the name for them.

data.title: Tank
data.definition: The name "Tank" is often associated with strength, power, and resilience, signifying a robust and formidable presence! This might be a good name for those pups with a strong and muscular physique, or just the kind that like to muscle in on any activity, no matter their size!

data.title: Tarzan
data.definition: Look up, you may see him swinging from the trees! Most famously used as the name for the child raised in the African jungle by the Mangani apes in the 1999 Disney film, Tarzan.

data.title: Tasha
data.definition: Tasha is a shortened form of 'Natasha', which has link to our favourite festive season as it means 'born on Christmas Day'! If you've got a winter-born pup on your hands, or just love a little reminder of that Christmas joy all year round, this might be the name for you!

data.title: Taz
data.definition: The name "Taz" is often associated with the popular Looney Tunes character, the Tasmanian Devil, so is full of energy, speed, and a spirited nature! If you've got a dog who is lively, playful, and full of enthusiasm, even if they're likely to leave a trail of destruction in their wake, Taz could be the right name!

data.title: Teddy
data.definition: This is one of the most popular dog names today, and with good reason. The name Teddy is a male name of French origin, meaning 'brave' and 'protector'. It can also sum up how fluffy and cuddly your pup is!

data.title: Tess
data.definition: Tess is of Greek origins and means 'summer' or 'to harvest'. The name "Tess" is a diminutive form of the name Theresa or Tessa, and might be associated with qualities such as gentleness, warmth, and kindness.

data.title: Thalia
data.definition: This is a joyful name, meaning 'dew from heaven' in Hebrew and 'to flourish' in Greek. Thalia was one of the Three Graces, known for the charm and beauty, and the Muse of Comedy. A great name for happy pets and those who like to play the clown!

data.title: Theo
data.definition: The name "Theo" is a shortened form of the name Theodore or Theodora, which has Greek origins. It is derived from the Greek words "theos" meaning "god" and "doron" meaning "gift." As such, the name Theo signifies a divine gift or a gift from God; perfect for a poock who is special, beloved, and brings joy to those around them.

data.title: Thistle
data.definition: A quirky name for fearless characters, Thistle literally means 'to prick' and is Scotland's national flower and symbol. A patriotic option for some of Scotland's feistiest hunters, like the Cairn Terrier, Border Terrier, or Dandie Dinmont Terrier.

data.title: Thor
data.definition: The name "Thor" originates from Norse mythology and is associated with the Norse god of thunder and strength. It signifies power, bravery, and a commanding presence and would be a good name for large dog breeds, and those that command attention!

data.title: Thumper
data.definition: This is a name that many hold close to their hearts, the name thumper is famous for being the name of the Rabbit from the childhood film Bambi. The characteristics associated with the name Thumper are clever, quick minded, and ambitious.

data.title: Tiana
data.definition: Another famous Disney character, Tiana is the princess in Disney's The Princess and the Frog. Perfect for a dog with a love of food!

data.title: Tigh
data.definition: A rocky tidal island off the coast of Scotland, Tigh is great name for rugged dogs with an independent, adventurous spirit!

data.title: Tilly
data.definition: Tilly is a lovely female name of German origin, which means 'mighty in battle'. This name is perfect for a powerful little pup with a free-spirited nature.

data.title: Timmy
data.definition: Another name to add to the popular list, Timmy was the fifth member of the Famous Five book series, written by Enid Blyton. The name Timmy means 'one who honours God'.

data.title: Timon
data.definition: Timon is a name of Greek origin, meaning 'respect' and 'honour' - made famous by the wise Meerkat and one half of the comedy double act, Timon and Pumbaa in Disney's The Lion King.

data.title: Tinker Bell
data.definition: Do you believe in fairies? Famous for being the protective and playful fairy friend in J.M. Barrie's Peter Pan, the name Tinker Bell is becoming ever popular as a pet name!

data.title: Titus
data.definition: For you ancient Rome lovers out there, chances are, you will know who this famous emperor is. The name means 'title of honour', and was used for the main character in the Shakespearean play Titus Andronicus. Titus is a great name for the new fluffy king in your life.

data.title: Tonto
data.definition: In Spanish this name means 'silly' or 'fool', and it was also the name of the resourceful Native American companion to the Lone Ranger, which in his language translated to 'wild one'. Tonto might suggest a canine companion who is devoted, clever, brings a sense of adventure to those around them and would always be by their owner's side.

data.title: Toto
data.definition: Toto is most famously known as Dorothy’s canine companion in The Wizard of Oz, played by a Cairn Terrier named Terry. Toto is Dorothy's loyal and faithful companion throughout her adventures in the Land of Oz making it the perfect name for your devoted little friend. It's actually a name of Greek origin, meaning ‘priest’.

data.title: Toulouse
data.definition: Toulouse - a beautiful place in France and a beautiful name for your pet!

data.title: Tramp
data.definition: Tramp is a name derived from a Middle English verb meaning to have 'heavy footsteps'. The name Tramp is famously used in the 1955 Disney film, Lady and the Tramp, and would be a great name if you have two dogs - a Lady and a Tramp!

data.title: Trapper
data.definition: We love the original German meaning of this name, which is 'mischief-maker' or 'rogue'! Great for those pups that can't help but get themselves into a little trouble - with a name like this, you've got to be one patient pet parent!

data.title: Tripp
data.definition: Do you have a clumsy pup? Or one with long limbs and no sense of balance? This is a great, comical name for them, literally meaning 'one who tripped'.

data.title: Trixie
data.definition: Trixie is girl's name of Latin origin, meaning 'bringer of joy'. What else do we need to say!

data.title: Tyson
data.definition: Tyson comes from French origins and means 'firebrand' - made famous by the notorious boxer Tyson Fury, this name is a great option for rough and tumble pups with spirited personalities!

data.title: Ukko
data.definition: The name Ukko, is of Finnish folk religion and is one of the most important deities. The name is derived from the word ukkonen, which means 'thunder'. It is used as a term of respect.

data.title: Ulf
data.definition: This is a great name for those who own a large dog such as a husky, or one with wolf-like features. The name Ulf is of Scandinavian and German origin, and comes from the Norse world for Űlfr meaning ‘wolf’.

data.title: Ursa
data.definition: Ursa as a girl's name is of Scandinavian and Latin origin, Ursa is derived from the Latin name Orsa, and it means 'little she-bear'.

data.title: Ursula
data.definition: If you have a small pooch that has sass and flare as a part of their personality, this name might be for you!Ursula is a name of Latin origin meaning 'little female bear'. Ursula has been used in Shakespeare's Two Gentlemen of Veron, as well as for the villain in Disney's The Little Mermaid.

data.title: Vanity
data.definition: Vanity is a girls name of English origin, the name means 'excessive pride'. Do you have a pet that is always very pleased with themselves? This is the perfect name for them.

data.title: Venus
data.definition: The Roman Goddess of love and beauty, Venus is also the name of the brightest planet in our sky.

data.title: Vicky
data.definition: The name "Vicky" is often considered a diminutive form of the name Victoria, which has Latin origins and means "victory" or "conqueror." I might be a good name for pups with a tenacious spirit and a drive to overcome challenges  - even if that's just how to convince you into a second dinner!

data.title: Victor
data.definition: The name "Victor" has Latin origins and is derived from the word "victor," which means "conqueror" or "winner." It might be a good choice for a canine companion who is strong, determined, and has a confident and assertive personality.

data.title: Vince
data.definition: A boy's name of Latin origin, and the meaning 'prevailing'. The name Vince is associated with being the short form of Vincent, but is becoming a popular given name choice. The most noteable holder of the name is Vincent Van Gogh.

data.title: Violet
data.definition: The name "Violet" is derived from the Latin word "viola," which refers to the flower of the same name and means 'purple'. It's often associated with creativity, spirituality, and elegance.

data.title: Wally
data.definition: Where's Wally? Let's hope you don't spend too much of your time asking about a little runaway! Wally has Germanic origins and means "ruler" or "powerful warrior", and might be a fun name choice for a pup who tends to camouflage amongst the outdoors, leaving you looking for them for the majority of the walk!

data.title: Walter
data.definition: The name "Walter" has Germanic origins and means "ruler of the army" or "powerful warrior." A great name for pups who are strong, confident, and have a determined personality.

data.title: Watson
data.definition: Looking for the ultimate sidekick in life? The Warson to your Holmes? Then this might be the name for your pup - get ready to solve some mysteries together, starting with "where are my socks?!"

data.title: Wendy
data.definition: Wendy is a female name of British origin, that means 'friend'. What better name to give your new four-legged friend? The name was chosen by J.M. Barrie for the leading chracter in the classic Peter Pan story.

data.title: Whisky
data.definition: This name means 'water of life', and of course refers to the popular Scottish alcohol distilled all over the country. If this floats your boat, you might also like to consider popular Scottish whiskies as names, like 'Ardy' (Ardbeg), 'Mac' (Macallan) or Glen (Glenfiddich).

data.title: Willow
data.definition: The name "Willow" is derived from the English word for the tree of the same name. The willow tree is known for its graceful and flexible branches, symbolizing resilience and adaptability. It might make a good name for long-legged breeds, those with extra lustrous coats, or adopted pups, as it's also seem a s a symbol of rebirth and new beginnings.

data.title: Winnie
data.definition: This name is perfect for the willy, nilly, silly pet in your life! Everyone knows Winnie The Pooh, the honey-loving bear from AA Milne's books, but did you know the name means 'Gentle Friend'?

data.title: Winston
data.definition: One of the most well-known names in history, for the mighty Winston Churchill. The name is of British origin and strangely, means 'joyful stone'. It's an absolute old-school classic, and about time for a revival!

data.title: Winter
data.definition: Wheter you are naming your pup after your favourite food, actor or season we have the names for you! Winter is a german female name that means 'time of water'.

data.title: Woody
data.definition: Sheriff Woody from Pixar's Toy Story is kind, courageous and a real leader of his pack. This is a great name for you Toy Story fans out there, as well as symbolsing your dogs kind, caring and loyal nature.

data.title: Wren
data.definition: The name "Wren" is derived from the Old English word "wrenna," which refers to a small, lively bird known for its melodious song. It might make a good choice for a spirited, agile, and joyful pup with a playful personality; or those likely to keep you entertained with their voice!

data.title: Xena
data.definition: The name "Xena" is often associated with the fictional character Xena, Warrior Princess, from the television series. It actually come from the Greek meaning 'guest' or 'stranger' - but what are strangers if not friends we just haven't met yet! Time to bring home your new best friend, and if you're looking for a name that's associated with strength, bravery, and a fierce warrior spirit, this might be the one for you!

data.title: Xia
data.definition: This is a girls name of Chinese origin that means 'glow of sunrise or sunset' this is a lovely name for a golden pup, or one with a heart of gold.

data.title: Xiáng
data.definition: Do you have a pup who you count as your lucky charm? Xiáng has many meanings associated with it, depending on the way it is written, but one of them is 'good luck'.

data.title: Yates
data.definition: Yates is primarily a gender-neutral name of English origin. Yates is a name that primarily is used as a surname, or as an ocupational name for someone that was known as a gate-keeper. The name means 'Dweller by a gate'.

data.title: Yeti
data.definition: The name "Yeti" is often associated with the mythical creature known as the Yeti or the Abominable Snowman, originating from the Himalayan region and known as a large, ape-like creature believed to inhabit the snowy mountains. If you like your names with a touch of imagination, this might be a good one for strong, adventurous, and of course, snow white pups!

data.title: Yoda
data.definition: The best-known character of all the Star Wars series and properties, Yoda is (see what we did there?). Yoda is the Jedi master who trains Luke Skywalker and is wise beyond his thousands of years—making this another solid choice for an older dog. Bringing home a puppy? Baby Yoda, from The Mandalorian is probably one of the cutest things we've ever seen and would totally work for a younger pup.

data.title: Yoshi
data.definition: The name "Yoshi" has Japanese origins and can have multiple meanings depending on the kanji characters used. One common interpretation is "good luck" or "lucky." Yoshi is also Mario and Luigi's pet dinosaur that appears in Nintendo video games - maybe you have a little T-Rex on your hands that needs an appropriately snappy name, or you just feel lucky to have found them.

data.title: Yue
data.definition: This is a really lovely name for those of you that own a black pup. The name means ‘born under the moon’, and would be a great choice for those gentle souls with a touch of moon magic about them!

data.title: Yzma
data.definition: A name of Arabic origin that means 'greatness', 'might', and 'importance'. Made most famous by Yzma the evil sorceress in Disney's The Emperor's New Groove. A great name for a great dog or one with a little magic in them!

data.title: Zane
data.definition: The name "Zane" has multiple origins and meanings. It can be derived from the Hebrew name "Zan," meaning "gift from God," or from the Arabic name "Zayn," meaning "beauty" or "grace."

data.title: Zara
data.definition: The name "Zara" has multiple origins and meanings across different cultures. In Arabic, Zara means "flower" or "blooming." In Hebrew, it means "princess" or "radiance."

data.title: Zelda
data.definition: The name "Zelda" has multiple origins and meanings. It can be derived from the Yiddish name "Selde," meaning "blessed" or "happy." It can also be associated with the German name "Griselda," meaning "gray battle" or "dark battle." We also tend to think of the Legend of Zelda vide game; a spirited and unique that brings a sense of adventure!

data.title: Zeus
data.definition: The name "Zeus" originates from Greek mythology and refers to the King of the Gods, God of the sky and thunder! Zeus was known for his power, strength, and authority so would make a good name for those strong-willed, confident pups ready to take on the world!

data.title: Ziggy
data.definition: The name "Ziggy" is often considered a diminutive form of the name "Zigfried" or "Siegfried" and is generally associated with a lively and energetic spirit!

data.title: Zinnia
data.definition: The zinnia flowers are known for their big, bright heads and symbolise 'remembrance'. As part of the sunflower family they're an energetic, optimistic bloom which would make an unexpected name for special pups!

data.title: Zoe
data.definition: The name "Zoe" has Greek origins and means "life" or "alive," suggesting a canine companion who is full of vitality, energy, and brings a sense of liveliness and joy to those around them!

data.title: Zola
data.definition: The name "Zola" has multiple origins and meanings. It can be derived from the Italian name "Zoilo," meaning "life" or "alive." It can also be associated with the African name "Zulu," meaning "quiet" or "peaceful." It would be a fitting choice for a dog owner seeking a name that reflects their furry friend's gentle and harmonious personality.

data.title: Zorro
data.definition: The Spanish meaning of Zorro is 'sly' or 'fox' - perfect for fox-type dogs like the Akita, Shiba Inu and various Spitzs!

data.title: Zuzu
data.definition: Meaning "lily" in Czech and "little pearl" in Chinese, there's something a little fun and feisty about the name Zuzu!
